imgui_draw.cpp
1// dear imgui, v1.92.7 WIP
2// (drawing and font code)
3
4/*
5
6Index of this file:
7
8// [SECTION] STB libraries implementation
9// [SECTION] Style functions
10// [SECTION] ImDrawList
11// [SECTION] ImTriangulator, ImDrawList concave polygon fill
12// [SECTION] ImDrawListSplitter
13// [SECTION] ImDrawData
14// [SECTION] Helpers ShadeVertsXXX functions
15// [SECTION] ImFontConfig
16// [SECTION] ImFontAtlas, ImFontAtlasBuilder
17// [SECTION] ImFontAtlas: backend for stb_truetype
18// [SECTION] ImFontAtlas: glyph ranges helpers
19// [SECTION] ImFontGlyphRangesBuilder
20// [SECTION] ImFontBaked, ImFont
21// [SECTION] ImGui Internal Render Helpers
22// [SECTION] Decompression code
23// [SECTION] Default font data (ProggyClean.ttf)
24// [SECTION] Default font data (ProggyForever.ttf)
25
26*/
27
28#if defined(_MSC_VER) && !defined(_CRT_SECURE_NO_WARNINGS)
29#define _CRT_SECURE_NO_WARNINGS
30#endif
31
32#ifndef IMGUI_DEFINE_MATH_OPERATORS
33#define IMGUI_DEFINE_MATH_OPERATORS
34#endif
35
36#include "imgui.h"
37#ifndef IMGUI_DISABLE
38#include "imgui_internal.h"
39#ifdef IMGUI_ENABLE_FREETYPE
40#include "misc/freetype/imgui_freetype.h"
41#endif
42
43#include <stdio.h> // vsnprintf, sscanf, printf
44#include <stdint.h> // intptr_t
45
46// Visual Studio warnings
47#ifdef _MSC_VER
48#pragma warning (disable: 4127) // condition expression is constant
49#pragma warning (disable: 4505) // unreferenced local function has been removed (stb stuff)
50#pragma warning (disable: 4996) // 'This function or variable may be unsafe': strcpy, strdup, sprintf, vsnprintf, sscanf, fopen
51#pragma warning (disable: 26451) // [Static Analyzer] Arithmetic overflow : Using operator 'xxx' on a 4 byte value and then casting the result to a 8 byte value. Cast the value to the wider type before calling operator 'xxx' to avoid overflow(io.2).
52#pragma warning (disable: 26812) // [Static Analyzer] The enum type 'xxx' is unscoped. Prefer 'enum class' over 'enum' (Enum.3).
53#endif
54
55// Clang/GCC warnings with -Weverything
56#if defined(__clang__)
57#if __has_warning("-Wunknown-warning-option")
58#pragma clang diagnostic ignored "-Wunknown-warning-option" // warning: unknown warning group 'xxx' // not all warnings are known by all Clang versions and they tend to be rename-happy.. so ignoring warnings triggers new warnings on some configuration. Great!
59#endif
60#pragma clang diagnostic ignored "-Wunknown-pragmas" // warning: unknown warning group 'xxx'
61#pragma clang diagnostic ignored "-Wold-style-cast" // warning: use of old-style cast // yes, they are more terse.
62#pragma clang diagnostic ignored "-Wfloat-equal" // warning: comparing floating point with == or != is unsafe // storing and comparing against same constants ok.
63#pragma clang diagnostic ignored "-Wglobal-constructors" // warning: declaration requires a global destructor // similar to above, not sure what the exact difference is.
64#pragma clang diagnostic ignored "-Wsign-conversion" // warning: implicit conversion changes signedness
65#pragma clang diagnostic ignored "-Wzero-as-null-pointer-constant" // warning: zero as null pointer constant // some standard header variations use #define NULL 0
66#pragma clang diagnostic ignored "-Wcomma" // warning: possible misuse of comma operator here
67#pragma clang diagnostic ignored "-Wreserved-id-macro" // warning: macro name is a reserved identifier
68#pragma clang diagnostic ignored "-Wdouble-promotion" // warning: implicit conversion from 'float' to 'double' when passing argument to function // using printf() is a misery with this as C++ va_arg ellipsis changes float to double.
69#pragma clang diagnostic ignored "-Wimplicit-int-float-conversion" // warning: implicit conversion from 'xxx' to 'float' may lose precision
70#pragma clang diagnostic ignored "-Wreserved-identifier" // warning: identifier '_Xxx' is reserved because it starts with '_' followed by a capital letter
71#pragma clang diagnostic ignored "-Wunsafe-buffer-usage" // warning: 'xxx' is an unsafe pointer used for buffer access
72#pragma clang diagnostic ignored "-Wnontrivial-memaccess" // warning: first argument in call to 'memset' is a pointer to non-trivially copyable type
73#pragma clang diagnostic ignored "-Wcast-qual" // warning: cast from 'const xxxx *' to 'xxx *' drops const qualifier
74#pragma clang diagnostic ignored "-Wswitch-default" // warning: 'switch' missing 'default' label
75#elif defined(__GNUC__)
76#pragma GCC diagnostic ignored "-Wpragmas" // warning: unknown option after '#pragma GCC diagnostic' kind
77#pragma GCC diagnostic ignored "-Wunused-function" // warning: 'xxxx' defined but not used
78#pragma GCC diagnostic ignored "-Wfloat-equal" // warning: comparing floating-point with '==' or '!=' is unsafe
79#pragma GCC diagnostic ignored "-Wdouble-promotion" // warning: implicit conversion from 'float' to 'double' when passing argument to function
80#pragma GCC diagnostic ignored "-Wconversion" // warning: conversion to 'xxxx' from 'xxxx' may alter its value
81#pragma GCC diagnostic ignored "-Wstack-protector" // warning: stack protector not protecting local variables: variable length buffer
82#pragma GCC diagnostic ignored "-Wstrict-overflow" // warning: assuming signed overflow does not occur when simplifying division / ..when changing X +- C1 cmp C2 to X cmp C2 -+ C1
83#pragma GCC diagnostic ignored "-Wclass-memaccess" // [__GNUC__ >= 8] warning: 'memset/memcpy' clearing/writing an object of type 'xxxx' with no trivial copy-assignment; use assignment or value-initialization instead
84#pragma GCC diagnostic ignored "-Wcast-qual" // warning: cast from type 'const xxxx *' to type 'xxxx *' casts away qualifiers
85#pragma GCC diagnostic ignored "-Wsign-conversion" // warning: conversion to 'xxxx' from 'xxxx' may change the sign of the result
86#endif
87
88//-------------------------------------------------------------------------
89// [SECTION] STB libraries implementation (for stb_truetype and stb_rect_pack)
90//-------------------------------------------------------------------------
91
92// Compile time options:
93//#define IMGUI_STB_NAMESPACE ImStb
94//#define IMGUI_STB_TRUETYPE_FILENAME "my_folder/stb_truetype.h"
95//#define IMGUI_STB_RECT_PACK_FILENAME "my_folder/stb_rect_pack.h"
96//#define IMGUI_DISABLE_STB_TRUETYPE_IMPLEMENTATION
97//#define IMGUI_DISABLE_STB_RECT_PACK_IMPLEMENTATION
98
99#ifdef IMGUI_STB_NAMESPACE
100namespace IMGUI_STB_NAMESPACE
101{
102#endif
103
104#ifdef _MSC_VER
105#pragma warning (push)
106#pragma warning (disable: 4456) // declaration of 'xx' hides previous local declaration
107#pragma warning (disable: 6011) // (stb_rectpack) Dereferencing NULL pointer 'cur->next'.
108#pragma warning (disable: 5262) // (stb_truetype) implicit fall-through occurs here; are you missing a break statement?
109#pragma warning (disable: 6385) // (stb_truetype) Reading invalid data from 'buffer': the readable size is '_Old_3`kernel_width' bytes, but '3' bytes may be read.
110#pragma warning (disable: 28182) // (stb_rectpack) Dereferencing NULL pointer. 'cur' contains the same NULL value as 'cur->next' did.
111#endif
112
113#if defined(__clang__)
114#pragma clang diagnostic push
115#pragma clang diagnostic ignored "-Wunused-function" // warning: 'xxxx' defined but not used
116#pragma clang diagnostic ignored "-Wmissing-prototypes"
117#pragma clang diagnostic ignored "-Wimplicit-fallthrough"
118#endif
119
120#if defined(__GNUC__)
121#pragma GCC diagnostic push
122#pragma GCC diagnostic ignored "-Wtype-limits" // warning: comparison is always true due to limited range of data type [-Wtype-limits]
123#pragma GCC diagnostic ignored "-Wimplicit-fallthrough" // warning: this statement may fall through
124#endif
125
126#ifndef STB_RECT_PACK_IMPLEMENTATION // in case the user already have an implementation in the _same_ compilation unit (e.g. unity builds)
127#ifndef IMGUI_DISABLE_STB_RECT_PACK_IMPLEMENTATION // in case the user already have an implementation in another compilation unit
128#define STBRP_STATIC
129#define STBRP_ASSERT(x) do { IM_ASSERT(x); } while (0)
130#define STBRP_SORT ImQsort
131#define STB_RECT_PACK_IMPLEMENTATION
132#endif
133#ifdef IMGUI_STB_RECT_PACK_FILENAME
134#include IMGUI_STB_RECT_PACK_FILENAME
135#else
136#include "imstb_rectpack.h"
137#endif
138#endif
139
140#ifdef IMGUI_ENABLE_STB_TRUETYPE
141#ifndef STB_TRUETYPE_IMPLEMENTATION // in case the user already have an implementation in the _same_ compilation unit (e.g. unity builds)
142#ifndef IMGUI_DISABLE_STB_TRUETYPE_IMPLEMENTATION // in case the user already have an implementation in another compilation unit
143#define STBTT_malloc(x,u) ((void)(u), IM_ALLOC(x))
144#define STBTT_free(x,u) ((void)(u), IM_FREE(x))
145#define STBTT_assert(x) do { IM_ASSERT(x); } while(0)
146#define STBTT_fmod(x,y) ImFmod(x,y)
147#define STBTT_sqrt(x) ImSqrt(x)
148#define STBTT_pow(x,y) ImPow(x,y)
149#define STBTT_fabs(x) ImFabs(x)
150#define STBTT_ifloor(x) ((int)ImFloor(x))
151#define STBTT_iceil(x) ((int)ImCeil(x))
152#define STBTT_strlen(x) ImStrlen(x)
153#define STBTT_STATIC
154#define STB_TRUETYPE_IMPLEMENTATION
155#else
156#define STBTT_DEF extern
157#endif
158#ifdef IMGUI_STB_TRUETYPE_FILENAME
159#include IMGUI_STB_TRUETYPE_FILENAME
160#else
161#include "imstb_truetype.h"
162#endif
163#endif
164#endif // IMGUI_ENABLE_STB_TRUETYPE
165
166#if defined(__GNUC__)
167#pragma GCC diagnostic pop
168#endif
169
170#if defined(__clang__)
171#pragma clang diagnostic pop
172#endif
173
174#if defined(_MSC_VER)
175#pragma warning (pop)
176#endif
177
178#ifdef IMGUI_STB_NAMESPACE
179} // namespace ImStb
180using namespace IMGUI_STB_NAMESPACE;
181#endif

183//-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
184// [SECTION] Style functions
185//-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
186
187void ImGui::StyleColorsDark(ImGuiStyle* dst)
188{
189 ImGuiStyle* style = dst ? dst : &ImGui::GetStyle();
190 ImVec4* colors = style->Colors;
191
192 colors[ImGuiCol_Text] = ImVec4(1.00f, 1.00f, 1.00f, 1.00f);
193 colors[ImGuiCol_TextDisabled] = ImVec4(0.50f, 0.50f, 0.50f, 1.00f);
194 colors[ImGuiCol_WindowBg] = ImVec4(0.06f, 0.06f, 0.06f, 0.94f);
195 colors[ImGuiCol_ChildBg] = ImVec4(0.00f, 0.00f, 0.00f, 0.00f);
196 colors[ImGuiCol_PopupBg] = ImVec4(0.08f, 0.08f, 0.08f, 0.94f);
197 colors[ImGuiCol_Border] = ImVec4(0.43f, 0.43f, 0.50f, 0.50f);
198 colors[ImGuiCol_BorderShadow] = ImVec4(0.00f, 0.00f, 0.00f, 0.00f);
199 colors[ImGuiCol_FrameBg] = ImVec4(0.16f, 0.29f, 0.48f, 0.54f);
200 colors[ImGuiCol_FrameBgHovered] = ImVec4(0.26f, 0.59f, 0.98f, 0.40f);
201 colors[ImGuiCol_FrameBgActive] = ImVec4(0.26f, 0.59f, 0.98f, 0.67f);
202 colors[ImGuiCol_TitleBg] = ImVec4(0.04f, 0.04f, 0.04f, 1.00f);
203 colors[ImGuiCol_TitleBgActive] = ImVec4(0.16f, 0.29f, 0.48f, 1.00f);
204 colors[ImGuiCol_TitleBgCollapsed] = ImVec4(0.00f, 0.00f, 0.00f, 0.51f);
205 colors[ImGuiCol_MenuBarBg] = ImVec4(0.14f, 0.14f, 0.14f, 1.00f);
206 colors[ImGuiCol_ScrollbarBg] = ImVec4(0.02f, 0.02f, 0.02f, 0.53f);
207 colors[ImGuiCol_ScrollbarGrab] = ImVec4(0.31f, 0.31f, 0.31f, 1.00f);
208 colors[ImGuiCol_ScrollbarGrabHovered] = ImVec4(0.41f, 0.41f, 0.41f, 1.00f);
209 colors[ImGuiCol_ScrollbarGrabActive] = ImVec4(0.51f, 0.51f, 0.51f, 1.00f);
210 colors[ImGuiCol_CheckMark] = ImVec4(0.26f, 0.59f, 0.98f, 1.00f);
211 colors[ImGuiCol_SliderGrab] = ImVec4(0.24f, 0.52f, 0.88f, 1.00f);
212 colors[ImGuiCol_SliderGrabActive] = ImVec4(0.26f, 0.59f, 0.98f, 1.00f);
213 colors[ImGuiCol_Button] = ImVec4(0.26f, 0.59f, 0.98f, 0.40f);
214 colors[ImGuiCol_ButtonHovered] = ImVec4(0.26f, 0.59f, 0.98f, 1.00f);
215 colors[ImGuiCol_ButtonActive] = ImVec4(0.06f, 0.53f, 0.98f, 1.00f);
216 colors[ImGuiCol_Header] = ImVec4(0.26f, 0.59f, 0.98f, 0.31f);
217 colors[ImGuiCol_HeaderHovered] = ImVec4(0.26f, 0.59f, 0.98f, 0.80f);
218 colors[ImGuiCol_HeaderActive] = ImVec4(0.26f, 0.59f, 0.98f, 1.00f);
219 colors[ImGuiCol_Separator] = colors[ImGuiCol_Border];
220 colors[ImGuiCol_SeparatorHovered] = ImVec4(0.10f, 0.40f, 0.75f, 0.78f);
221 colors[ImGuiCol_SeparatorActive] = ImVec4(0.10f, 0.40f, 0.75f, 1.00f);
222 colors[ImGuiCol_ResizeGrip] = ImVec4(0.26f, 0.59f, 0.98f, 0.20f);
223 colors[ImGuiCol_ResizeGripHovered] = ImVec4(0.26f, 0.59f, 0.98f, 0.67f);
224 colors[ImGuiCol_ResizeGripActive] = ImVec4(0.26f, 0.59f, 0.98f, 0.95f);
225 colors[ImGuiCol_InputTextCursor] = colors[ImGuiCol_Text];
226 colors[ImGuiCol_TabHovered] = colors[ImGuiCol_HeaderHovered];
227 colors[ImGuiCol_Tab] = ImLerp(colors[ImGuiCol_Header], colors[ImGuiCol_TitleBgActive], 0.80f);
228 colors[ImGuiCol_TabSelected] = ImLerp(colors[ImGuiCol_HeaderActive], colors[ImGuiCol_TitleBgActive], 0.60f);
229 colors[ImGuiCol_TabSelectedOverline] = colors[ImGuiCol_HeaderActive];
230 colors[ImGuiCol_TabDimmed] = ImLerp(colors[ImGuiCol_Tab], colors[ImGuiCol_TitleBg], 0.80f);
231 colors[ImGuiCol_TabDimmedSelected] = ImLerp(colors[ImGuiCol_TabSelected], colors[ImGuiCol_TitleBg], 0.40f);
232 colors[ImGuiCol_TabDimmedSelectedOverline] = ImVec4(0.50f, 0.50f, 0.50f, 0.00f);
233 colors[ImGuiCol_DockingPreview] = colors[ImGuiCol_HeaderActive] * ImVec4(1.0f, 1.0f, 1.0f, 0.7f);
234 colors[ImGuiCol_DockingEmptyBg] = ImVec4(0.20f, 0.20f, 0.20f, 1.00f);
235 colors[ImGuiCol_PlotLines] = ImVec4(0.61f, 0.61f, 0.61f, 1.00f);
236 colors[ImGuiCol_PlotLinesHovered] = ImVec4(1.00f, 0.43f, 0.35f, 1.00f);
237 colors[ImGuiCol_PlotHistogram] = ImVec4(0.90f, 0.70f, 0.00f, 1.00f);
238 colors[ImGuiCol_PlotHistogramHovered] = ImVec4(1.00f, 0.60f, 0.00f, 1.00f);
239 colors[ImGuiCol_TableHeaderBg] = ImVec4(0.19f, 0.19f, 0.20f, 1.00f);
240 colors[ImGuiCol_TableBorderStrong] = ImVec4(0.31f, 0.31f, 0.35f, 1.00f); // Prefer using Alpha=1.0 here
241 colors[ImGuiCol_TableBorderLight] = ImVec4(0.23f, 0.23f, 0.25f, 1.00f); // Prefer using Alpha=1.0 here
242 colors[ImGuiCol_TableRowBg] = ImVec4(0.00f, 0.00f, 0.00f, 0.00f);
243 colors[ImGuiCol_TableRowBgAlt] = ImVec4(1.00f, 1.00f, 1.00f, 0.06f);
244 colors[ImGuiCol_TextLink] = colors[ImGuiCol_HeaderActive];
245 colors[ImGuiCol_TextSelectedBg] = ImVec4(0.26f, 0.59f, 0.98f, 0.35f);
246 colors[ImGuiCol_TreeLines] = colors[ImGuiCol_Border];
247 colors[ImGuiCol_DragDropTarget] = ImVec4(1.00f, 1.00f, 0.00f, 0.90f);
248 colors[ImGuiCol_DragDropTargetBg] = ImVec4(0.00f, 0.00f, 0.00f, 0.00f);
249 colors[ImGuiCol_UnsavedMarker] = ImVec4(1.00f, 1.00f, 1.00f, 1.00f);
250 colors[ImGuiCol_NavCursor] = ImVec4(0.26f, 0.59f, 0.98f, 1.00f);
251 colors[ImGuiCol_NavWindowingHighlight] = ImVec4(1.00f, 1.00f, 1.00f, 0.70f);
252 colors[ImGuiCol_NavWindowingDimBg] = ImVec4(0.80f, 0.80f, 0.80f, 0.20f);
253 colors[ImGuiCol_ModalWindowDimBg] = ImVec4(0.80f, 0.80f, 0.80f, 0.35f);
254}

256void ImGui::StyleColorsClassic(ImGuiStyle* dst)
257{
258 ImGuiStyle* style = dst ? dst : &ImGui::GetStyle();
259 ImVec4* colors = style->Colors;
260
261 colors[ImGuiCol_Text] = ImVec4(0.90f, 0.90f, 0.90f, 1.00f);
262 colors[ImGuiCol_TextDisabled] = ImVec4(0.60f, 0.60f, 0.60f, 1.00f);
263 colors[ImGuiCol_WindowBg] = ImVec4(0.00f, 0.00f, 0.00f, 0.85f);
264 colors[ImGuiCol_ChildBg] = ImVec4(0.00f, 0.00f, 0.00f, 0.00f);
265 colors[ImGuiCol_PopupBg] = ImVec4(0.11f, 0.11f, 0.14f, 0.92f);
266 colors[ImGuiCol_Border] = ImVec4(0.50f, 0.50f, 0.50f, 0.50f);
267 colors[ImGuiCol_BorderShadow] = ImVec4(0.00f, 0.00f, 0.00f, 0.00f);
268 colors[ImGuiCol_FrameBg] = ImVec4(0.43f, 0.43f, 0.43f, 0.39f);
269 colors[ImGuiCol_FrameBgHovered] = ImVec4(0.47f, 0.47f, 0.69f, 0.40f);
270 colors[ImGuiCol_FrameBgActive] = ImVec4(0.42f, 0.41f, 0.64f, 0.69f);
271 colors[ImGuiCol_TitleBg] = ImVec4(0.27f, 0.27f, 0.54f, 0.83f);
272 colors[ImGuiCol_TitleBgActive] = ImVec4(0.32f, 0.32f, 0.63f, 0.87f);
273 colors[ImGuiCol_TitleBgCollapsed] = ImVec4(0.40f, 0.40f, 0.80f, 0.20f);
274 colors[ImGuiCol_MenuBarBg] = ImVec4(0.40f, 0.40f, 0.55f, 0.80f);
275 colors[ImGuiCol_ScrollbarBg] = ImVec4(0.20f, 0.25f, 0.30f, 0.60f);
276 colors[ImGuiCol_ScrollbarGrab] = ImVec4(0.40f, 0.40f, 0.80f, 0.30f);
277 colors[ImGuiCol_ScrollbarGrabHovered] = ImVec4(0.40f, 0.40f, 0.80f, 0.40f);
278 colors[ImGuiCol_ScrollbarGrabActive] = ImVec4(0.41f, 0.39f, 0.80f, 0.60f);
279 colors[ImGuiCol_CheckMark] = ImVec4(0.90f, 0.90f, 0.90f, 0.50f);
280 colors[ImGuiCol_SliderGrab] = ImVec4(1.00f, 1.00f, 1.00f, 0.30f);
281 colors[ImGuiCol_SliderGrabActive] = ImVec4(0.41f, 0.39f, 0.80f, 0.60f);
282 colors[ImGuiCol_Button] = ImVec4(0.35f, 0.40f, 0.61f, 0.62f);
283 colors[ImGuiCol_ButtonHovered] = ImVec4(0.40f, 0.48f, 0.71f, 0.79f);
284 colors[ImGuiCol_ButtonActive] = ImVec4(0.46f, 0.54f, 0.80f, 1.00f);
285 colors[ImGuiCol_Header] = ImVec4(0.40f, 0.40f, 0.90f, 0.45f);
286 colors[ImGuiCol_HeaderHovered] = ImVec4(0.45f, 0.45f, 0.90f, 0.80f);
287 colors[ImGuiCol_HeaderActive] = ImVec4(0.53f, 0.53f, 0.87f, 0.80f);
288 colors[ImGuiCol_Separator] = ImVec4(0.50f, 0.50f, 0.50f, 0.60f);
289 colors[ImGuiCol_SeparatorHovered] = ImVec4(0.60f, 0.60f, 0.70f, 1.00f);
290 colors[ImGuiCol_SeparatorActive] = ImVec4(0.70f, 0.70f, 0.90f, 1.00f);
291 colors[ImGuiCol_ResizeGrip] = ImVec4(1.00f, 1.00f, 1.00f, 0.10f);
292 colors[ImGuiCol_ResizeGripHovered] = ImVec4(0.78f, 0.82f, 1.00f, 0.60f);
293 colors[ImGuiCol_ResizeGripActive] = ImVec4(0.78f, 0.82f, 1.00f, 0.90f);
294 colors[ImGuiCol_InputTextCursor] = colors[ImGuiCol_Text];
295 colors[ImGuiCol_TabHovered] = colors[ImGuiCol_HeaderHovered];
296 colors[ImGuiCol_Tab] = ImLerp(colors[ImGuiCol_Header], colors[ImGuiCol_TitleBgActive], 0.80f);
297 colors[ImGuiCol_TabSelected] = ImLerp(colors[ImGuiCol_HeaderActive], colors[ImGuiCol_TitleBgActive], 0.60f);
298 colors[ImGuiCol_TabSelectedOverline] = colors[ImGuiCol_HeaderActive];
299 colors[ImGuiCol_TabDimmed] = ImLerp(colors[ImGuiCol_Tab], colors[ImGuiCol_TitleBg], 0.80f);
300 colors[ImGuiCol_TabDimmedSelected] = ImLerp(colors[ImGuiCol_TabSelected], colors[ImGuiCol_TitleBg], 0.40f);
301 colors[ImGuiCol_TabDimmedSelectedOverline] = ImVec4(0.53f, 0.53f, 0.87f, 0.00f);
302 colors[ImGuiCol_DockingPreview] = colors[ImGuiCol_Header] * ImVec4(1.0f, 1.0f, 1.0f, 0.7f);
303 colors[ImGuiCol_DockingEmptyBg] = ImVec4(0.20f, 0.20f, 0.20f, 1.00f);
304 colors[ImGuiCol_PlotLines] = ImVec4(1.00f, 1.00f, 1.00f, 1.00f);
305 colors[ImGuiCol_PlotLinesHovered] = ImVec4(0.90f, 0.70f, 0.00f, 1.00f);
306 colors[ImGuiCol_PlotHistogram] = ImVec4(0.90f, 0.70f, 0.00f, 1.00f);
307 colors[ImGuiCol_PlotHistogramHovered] = ImVec4(1.00f, 0.60f, 0.00f, 1.00f);
308 colors[ImGuiCol_TableHeaderBg] = ImVec4(0.27f, 0.27f, 0.38f, 1.00f);
309 colors[ImGuiCol_TableBorderStrong] = ImVec4(0.31f, 0.31f, 0.45f, 1.00f); // Prefer using Alpha=1.0 here
310 colors[ImGuiCol_TableBorderLight] = ImVec4(0.26f, 0.26f, 0.28f, 1.00f); // Prefer using Alpha=1.0 here
311 colors[ImGuiCol_TableRowBg] = ImVec4(0.00f, 0.00f, 0.00f, 0.00f);
312 colors[ImGuiCol_TableRowBgAlt] = ImVec4(1.00f, 1.00f, 1.00f, 0.07f);
313 colors[ImGuiCol_TextLink] = colors[ImGuiCol_HeaderActive];
314 colors[ImGuiCol_TextSelectedBg] = ImVec4(0.00f, 0.00f, 1.00f, 0.35f);
315 colors[ImGuiCol_TreeLines] = colors[ImGuiCol_Border];
316 colors[ImGuiCol_DragDropTarget] = ImVec4(1.00f, 1.00f, 0.00f, 0.90f);
317 colors[ImGuiCol_DragDropTargetBg] = ImVec4(0.00f, 0.00f, 0.00f, 0.00f);
318 colors[ImGuiCol_UnsavedMarker] = ImVec4(0.90f, 0.90f, 0.90f, 1.00f);
319 colors[ImGuiCol_NavCursor] = colors[ImGuiCol_HeaderHovered];
320 colors[ImGuiCol_NavWindowingHighlight] = ImVec4(1.00f, 1.00f, 1.00f, 0.70f);
321 colors[ImGuiCol_NavWindowingDimBg] = ImVec4(0.80f, 0.80f, 0.80f, 0.20f);
322 colors[ImGuiCol_ModalWindowDimBg] = ImVec4(0.20f, 0.20f, 0.20f, 0.35f);
323}
324
325// Those light colors are better suited with a thicker font than the default one + FrameBorder
326void ImGui::StyleColorsLight(ImGuiStyle* dst)
327{
328 ImGuiStyle* style = dst ? dst : &ImGui::GetStyle();
329 ImVec4* colors = style->Colors;
330
331 colors[ImGuiCol_Text] = ImVec4(0.00f, 0.00f, 0.00f, 1.00f);
332 colors[ImGuiCol_TextDisabled] = ImVec4(0.60f, 0.60f, 0.60f, 1.00f);
333 colors[ImGuiCol_WindowBg] = ImVec4(0.94f, 0.94f, 0.94f, 1.00f);
334 colors[ImGuiCol_ChildBg] = ImVec4(0.00f, 0.00f, 0.00f, 0.00f);
335 colors[ImGuiCol_PopupBg] = ImVec4(1.00f, 1.00f, 1.00f, 0.98f);
336 colors[ImGuiCol_Border] = ImVec4(0.00f, 0.00f, 0.00f, 0.30f);
337 colors[ImGuiCol_BorderShadow] = ImVec4(0.00f, 0.00f, 0.00f, 0.00f);
338 colors[ImGuiCol_FrameBg] = ImVec4(1.00f, 1.00f, 1.00f, 1.00f);
339 colors[ImGuiCol_FrameBgHovered] = ImVec4(0.26f, 0.59f, 0.98f, 0.40f);
340 colors[ImGuiCol_FrameBgActive] = ImVec4(0.26f, 0.59f, 0.98f, 0.67f);
341 colors[ImGuiCol_TitleBg] = ImVec4(0.96f, 0.96f, 0.96f, 1.00f);
342 colors[ImGuiCol_TitleBgActive] = ImVec4(0.82f, 0.82f, 0.82f, 1.00f);
343 colors[ImGuiCol_TitleBgCollapsed] = ImVec4(1.00f, 1.00f, 1.00f, 0.51f);
344 colors[ImGuiCol_MenuBarBg] = ImVec4(0.86f, 0.86f, 0.86f, 1.00f);
345 colors[ImGuiCol_ScrollbarBg] = ImVec4(0.98f, 0.98f, 0.98f, 0.53f);
346 colors[ImGuiCol_ScrollbarGrab] = ImVec4(0.69f, 0.69f, 0.69f, 0.80f);
347 colors[ImGuiCol_ScrollbarGrabHovered] = ImVec4(0.49f, 0.49f, 0.49f, 0.80f);
348 colors[ImGuiCol_ScrollbarGrabActive] = ImVec4(0.49f, 0.49f, 0.49f, 1.00f);
349 colors[ImGuiCol_CheckMark] = ImVec4(0.26f, 0.59f, 0.98f, 1.00f);
350 colors[ImGuiCol_SliderGrab] = ImVec4(0.26f, 0.59f, 0.98f, 0.78f);
351 colors[ImGuiCol_SliderGrabActive] = ImVec4(0.46f, 0.54f, 0.80f, 0.60f);
352 colors[ImGuiCol_Button] = ImVec4(0.26f, 0.59f, 0.98f, 0.40f);
353 colors[ImGuiCol_ButtonHovered] = ImVec4(0.26f, 0.59f, 0.98f, 1.00f);
354 colors[ImGuiCol_ButtonActive] = ImVec4(0.06f, 0.53f, 0.98f, 1.00f);
355 colors[ImGuiCol_Header] = ImVec4(0.26f, 0.59f, 0.98f, 0.31f);
356 colors[ImGuiCol_HeaderHovered] = ImVec4(0.26f, 0.59f, 0.98f, 0.80f);
357 colors[ImGuiCol_HeaderActive] = ImVec4(0.26f, 0.59f, 0.98f, 1.00f);
358 colors[ImGuiCol_Separator] = ImVec4(0.39f, 0.39f, 0.39f, 0.62f);
359 colors[ImGuiCol_SeparatorHovered] = ImVec4(0.14f, 0.44f, 0.80f, 0.78f);
360 colors[ImGuiCol_SeparatorActive] = ImVec4(0.14f, 0.44f, 0.80f, 1.00f);
361 colors[ImGuiCol_ResizeGrip] = ImVec4(0.35f, 0.35f, 0.35f, 0.17f);
362 colors[ImGuiCol_ResizeGripHovered] = ImVec4(0.26f, 0.59f, 0.98f, 0.67f);
363 colors[ImGuiCol_ResizeGripActive] = ImVec4(0.26f, 0.59f, 0.98f, 0.95f);
364 colors[ImGuiCol_InputTextCursor] = colors[ImGuiCol_Text];
365 colors[ImGuiCol_TabHovered] = colors[ImGuiCol_HeaderHovered];
366 colors[ImGuiCol_Tab] = ImLerp(colors[ImGuiCol_Header], colors[ImGuiCol_TitleBgActive], 0.90f);
367 colors[ImGuiCol_TabSelected] = ImLerp(colors[ImGuiCol_HeaderActive], colors[ImGuiCol_TitleBgActive], 0.60f);
368 colors[ImGuiCol_TabSelectedOverline] = colors[ImGuiCol_HeaderActive];
369 colors[ImGuiCol_TabDimmed] = ImLerp(colors[ImGuiCol_Tab], colors[ImGuiCol_TitleBg], 0.80f);
370 colors[ImGuiCol_TabDimmedSelected] = ImLerp(colors[ImGuiCol_TabSelected], colors[ImGuiCol_TitleBg], 0.40f);
371 colors[ImGuiCol_TabDimmedSelectedOverline] = ImVec4(0.26f, 0.59f, 1.00f, 0.00f);
372 colors[ImGuiCol_DockingPreview] = colors[ImGuiCol_Header] * ImVec4(1.0f, 1.0f, 1.0f, 0.7f);
373 colors[ImGuiCol_DockingEmptyBg] = ImVec4(0.20f, 0.20f, 0.20f, 1.00f);
374 colors[ImGuiCol_PlotLines] = ImVec4(0.39f, 0.39f, 0.39f, 1.00f);
375 colors[ImGuiCol_PlotLinesHovered] = ImVec4(1.00f, 0.43f, 0.35f, 1.00f);
376 colors[ImGuiCol_PlotHistogram] = ImVec4(0.90f, 0.70f, 0.00f, 1.00f);
377 colors[ImGuiCol_PlotHistogramHovered] = ImVec4(1.00f, 0.45f, 0.00f, 1.00f);
378 colors[ImGuiCol_TableHeaderBg] = ImVec4(0.78f, 0.87f, 0.98f, 1.00f);
379 colors[ImGuiCol_TableBorderStrong] = ImVec4(0.57f, 0.57f, 0.64f, 1.00f); // Prefer using Alpha=1.0 here
380 colors[ImGuiCol_TableBorderLight] = ImVec4(0.68f, 0.68f, 0.74f, 1.00f); // Prefer using Alpha=1.0 here
381 colors[ImGuiCol_TableRowBg] = ImVec4(0.00f, 0.00f, 0.00f, 0.00f);
382 colors[ImGuiCol_TableRowBgAlt] = ImVec4(0.30f, 0.30f, 0.30f, 0.09f);
383 colors[ImGuiCol_TextLink] = colors[ImGuiCol_HeaderActive];
384 colors[ImGuiCol_TextSelectedBg] = ImVec4(0.26f, 0.59f, 0.98f, 0.35f);
385 colors[ImGuiCol_TreeLines] = colors[ImGuiCol_Border];
386 colors[ImGuiCol_DragDropTarget] = ImVec4(0.26f, 0.59f, 0.98f, 0.95f);
387 colors[ImGuiCol_DragDropTargetBg] = ImVec4(0.00f, 0.00f, 0.00f, 0.00f);
388 colors[ImGuiCol_UnsavedMarker] = ImVec4(0.00f, 0.00f, 0.00f, 1.00f);
389 colors[ImGuiCol_NavCursor] = colors[ImGuiCol_HeaderHovered];
390 colors[ImGuiCol_NavWindowingHighlight] = ImVec4(0.70f, 0.70f, 0.70f, 0.70f);
391 colors[ImGuiCol_NavWindowingDimBg] = ImVec4(0.20f, 0.20f, 0.20f, 0.20f);
392 colors[ImGuiCol_ModalWindowDimBg] = ImVec4(0.20f, 0.20f, 0.20f, 0.35f);
393}
394
395//-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
396// [SECTION] ImDrawList
397//-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
398
399ImDrawListSharedData::ImDrawListSharedData()
400{
401 memset((void*)this, 0, sizeof(*this));
402 InitialFringeScale = 1.0f;
403 for (int i = 0; i < IM_COUNTOF(ArcFastVtx); i++)
404 {
405 const float a = ((float)i * 2 * IM_PI) / (float)IM_COUNTOF(ArcFastVtx);
406 ArcFastVtx[i] = ImVec2(ImCos(a), ImSin(a));
407 }
408 ArcFastRadiusCutoff = IM_DRAWLIST_CIRCLE_AUTO_SEGMENT_CALC_R(IM_DRAWLIST_ARCFAST_SAMPLE_MAX, CircleSegmentMaxError);
409}
410
411ImDrawListSharedData::~ImDrawListSharedData()
412{
413 IM_ASSERT(DrawLists.Size == 0);
414}
415
416void ImDrawListSharedData::SetCircleTessellationMaxError(float max_error)
417{
418 if (CircleSegmentMaxError == max_error)
419 return;
420
421 IM_ASSERT(max_error > 0.0f);
422 CircleSegmentMaxError = max_error;
423 for (int i = 0; i < IM_COUNTOF(CircleSegmentCounts); i++)
424 {
425 const float radius = (float)i;
426 CircleSegmentCounts[i] = (ImU8)((i > 0) ? IM_DRAWLIST_CIRCLE_AUTO_SEGMENT_CALC(radius, CircleSegmentMaxError) : IM_DRAWLIST_ARCFAST_SAMPLE_MAX);
427 }
428 ArcFastRadiusCutoff = IM_DRAWLIST_CIRCLE_AUTO_SEGMENT_CALC_R(IM_DRAWLIST_ARCFAST_SAMPLE_MAX, CircleSegmentMaxError);
429}
430
431ImDrawList::ImDrawList(ImDrawListSharedData* shared_data)
432{
433 memset((void*)this, 0, sizeof(*this));
434 _SetDrawListSharedData(shared_data);
435}
436
437ImDrawList::~ImDrawList()
438{
439 _ClearFreeMemory();
440 _SetDrawListSharedData(NULL);
441}
442
443void ImDrawList::_SetDrawListSharedData(ImDrawListSharedData* data)
444{
445 if (_Data != NULL)
446 _Data->DrawLists.find_erase_unsorted(this);
447 _Data = data;
448 if (_Data != NULL)
449 _Data->DrawLists.push_back(this);
450}
451
452// Initialize before use in a new frame. We always have a command ready in the buffer.
453// In the majority of cases, you would want to call PushClipRect() and PushTexture() after this.
454void ImDrawList::_ResetForNewFrame()
455{
456 // Verify that the ImDrawCmd fields we want to memcmp() are contiguous in memory to match ImDrawCmdHeader.
457 IM_STATIC_ASSERT(offsetof(ImDrawCmd, ClipRect) == 0);
458 IM_STATIC_ASSERT(offsetof(ImDrawCmd, TexRef) == sizeof(ImVec4));
459 IM_STATIC_ASSERT(offsetof(ImDrawCmd, VtxOffset) == sizeof(ImVec4) + sizeof(ImTextureRef));
460 IM_STATIC_ASSERT(offsetof(ImDrawCmd, ClipRect) == offsetof(ImDrawCmdHeader, ClipRect));
461 IM_STATIC_ASSERT(offsetof(ImDrawCmd, TexRef) == offsetof(ImDrawCmdHeader, TexRef));
462 IM_STATIC_ASSERT(offsetof(ImDrawCmd, VtxOffset) == offsetof(ImDrawCmdHeader, VtxOffset));
463 if (_Splitter._Count > 1)
464 _Splitter.Merge(this);
465
466 CmdBuffer.resize(0);
467 IdxBuffer.resize(0);
468 VtxBuffer.resize(0);
469 Flags = _Data->InitialFlags;
470 memset(&_CmdHeader, 0, sizeof(_CmdHeader));
471 _VtxCurrentIdx = 0;
472 _VtxWritePtr = NULL;
473 _IdxWritePtr = NULL;
474 _ClipRectStack.resize(0);
475 _TextureStack.resize(0);
476 _CallbacksDataBuf.resize(0);
477 _Path.resize(0);
478 _Splitter.Clear();
479 CmdBuffer.push_back(ImDrawCmd());
480 _FringeScale = _Data->InitialFringeScale;
481}
482
483void ImDrawList::_ClearFreeMemory()
484{
485 CmdBuffer.clear();
486 IdxBuffer.clear();
487 VtxBuffer.clear();
488 Flags = ImDrawListFlags_None;
489 _VtxCurrentIdx = 0;
490 _VtxWritePtr = NULL;
491 _IdxWritePtr = NULL;
492 _ClipRectStack.clear();
493 _TextureStack.clear();
494 _CallbacksDataBuf.clear();
495 _Path.clear();
496 _Splitter.ClearFreeMemory();
497}
498
499// Note: For multi-threaded rendering, consider using `imgui_threaded_rendering` from https://github.com/ocornut/imgui_club
500ImDrawList* ImDrawList::CloneOutput() const
501{
502 ImDrawList* dst = IM_NEW(ImDrawList(NULL));
503 dst->CmdBuffer = CmdBuffer;
504 dst->IdxBuffer = IdxBuffer;
505 dst->VtxBuffer = VtxBuffer;
506 dst->Flags = Flags;
507 return dst;
508}
509
510void ImDrawList::AddDrawCmd()
511{
512 ImDrawCmd draw_cmd;
513 draw_cmd.ClipRect = _CmdHeader.ClipRect; // Same as calling ImDrawCmd_HeaderCopy()
514 draw_cmd.TexRef = _CmdHeader.TexRef;
515 draw_cmd.VtxOffset = _CmdHeader.VtxOffset;
516 draw_cmd.IdxOffset = IdxBuffer.Size;
517
518 IM_ASSERT(draw_cmd.ClipRect.x <= draw_cmd.ClipRect.z && draw_cmd.ClipRect.y <= draw_cmd.ClipRect.w);
519 CmdBuffer.push_back(draw_cmd);
520}
521
522// Pop trailing draw command (used before merging or presenting to user)
523// Note that this leaves the ImDrawList in a state unfit for further commands, as most code assume that CmdBuffer.Size > 0 && CmdBuffer.back().UserCallback == NULL
524void ImDrawList::_PopUnusedDrawCmd()
525{
526 while (CmdBuffer.Size > 0)
527 {
528 ImDrawCmd* curr_cmd = &CmdBuffer.Data[CmdBuffer.Size - 1];
529 if (curr_cmd->ElemCount != 0 || curr_cmd->UserCallback != NULL)
530 return;// break;
531 CmdBuffer.pop_back();
532 }
533}
534
535void ImDrawList::AddCallback(ImDrawCallback callback, void* userdata, size_t userdata_size)
536{
537 IM_ASSERT_PARANOID(CmdBuffer.Size > 0);
538 ImDrawCmd* curr_cmd = &CmdBuffer.Data[CmdBuffer.Size - 1];
539 IM_ASSERT(callback != NULL);
540 IM_ASSERT(curr_cmd->UserCallback == NULL);
541 if (curr_cmd->ElemCount != 0)
542 {
543 AddDrawCmd();
544 curr_cmd = &CmdBuffer.Data[CmdBuffer.Size - 1];
545 }
546
547 curr_cmd->UserCallback = callback;
548 if (userdata_size == 0)
549 {
550 // Store user data directly in command (no indirection)
551 curr_cmd->UserCallbackData = userdata;
552 curr_cmd->UserCallbackDataSize = 0;
553 curr_cmd->UserCallbackDataOffset = -1;
554 }
555 else
556 {
557 // Copy and store user data in a buffer
558 IM_ASSERT(userdata != NULL);
559 IM_ASSERT(userdata_size < (1u << 31));
560 curr_cmd->UserCallbackData = NULL; // Will be resolved during Render()
561 curr_cmd->UserCallbackDataSize = (int)userdata_size;
562 curr_cmd->UserCallbackDataOffset = _CallbacksDataBuf.Size;
563 _CallbacksDataBuf.resize(_CallbacksDataBuf.Size + (int)userdata_size);
564 memcpy(_CallbacksDataBuf.Data + (size_t)curr_cmd->UserCallbackDataOffset, userdata, userdata_size);
565 }
566
567 AddDrawCmd(); // Force a new command after us (see comment below)
568}
569
570// Compare ClipRect, TexRef and VtxOffset with a single memcmp()
571#define ImDrawCmd_HeaderSize (offsetof(ImDrawCmd, VtxOffset) + sizeof(unsigned int))
572#define ImDrawCmd_HeaderCompare(CMD_LHS, CMD_RHS) (memcmp(CMD_LHS, CMD_RHS, ImDrawCmd_HeaderSize)) // Compare ClipRect, TexRef, VtxOffset
573#define ImDrawCmd_HeaderCopy(CMD_DST, CMD_SRC) (memcpy(CMD_DST, CMD_SRC, ImDrawCmd_HeaderSize)) // Copy ClipRect, TexRef, VtxOffset
574#define ImDrawCmd_AreSequentialIdxOffset(CMD_0, CMD_1) (CMD_0->IdxOffset + CMD_0->ElemCount == CMD_1->IdxOffset)
575
576// Try to merge two last draw commands
577void ImDrawList::_TryMergeDrawCmds()
578{
579 IM_ASSERT_PARANOID(CmdBuffer.Size > 0);
580 ImDrawCmd* curr_cmd = &CmdBuffer.Data[CmdBuffer.Size - 1];
581 ImDrawCmd* prev_cmd = curr_cmd - 1;
582 if (ImDrawCmd_HeaderCompare(curr_cmd, prev_cmd) == 0 && ImDrawCmd_AreSequentialIdxOffset(prev_cmd, curr_cmd) && curr_cmd->UserCallback == NULL && prev_cmd->UserCallback == NULL)
583 {
584 prev_cmd->ElemCount += curr_cmd->ElemCount;
585 CmdBuffer.pop_back();
586 }
587}
588
589// Our scheme may appears a bit unusual, basically we want the most-common calls AddLine AddRect etc. to not have to perform any check so we always have a command ready in the stack.
590// The cost of figuring out if a new command has to be added or if we can merge is paid in those Update** functions only.
591void ImDrawList::_OnChangedClipRect()
592{
593 // If current command is used with different settings we need to add a new command
594 IM_ASSERT_PARANOID(CmdBuffer.Size > 0);
595 ImDrawCmd* curr_cmd = &CmdBuffer.Data[CmdBuffer.Size - 1];
596 if (curr_cmd->ElemCount != 0 && memcmp(&curr_cmd->ClipRect, &_CmdHeader.ClipRect, sizeof(ImVec4)) != 0)
597 {
598 AddDrawCmd();
599 return;
600 }
601 IM_ASSERT(curr_cmd->UserCallback == NULL);
602
603 // Try to merge with previous command if it matches, else use current command
604 ImDrawCmd* prev_cmd = curr_cmd - 1;
605 if (curr_cmd->ElemCount == 0 && CmdBuffer.Size > 1 && ImDrawCmd_HeaderCompare(&_CmdHeader, prev_cmd) == 0 && ImDrawCmd_AreSequentialIdxOffset(prev_cmd, curr_cmd) && prev_cmd->UserCallback == NULL)
606 {
607 CmdBuffer.pop_back();
608 return;
609 }
610 curr_cmd->ClipRect = _CmdHeader.ClipRect;
611}
612
613void ImDrawList::_OnChangedTexture()
614{
615 // If current command is used with different settings we need to add a new command
616 IM_ASSERT_PARANOID(CmdBuffer.Size > 0);
617 ImDrawCmd* curr_cmd = &CmdBuffer.Data[CmdBuffer.Size - 1];
618 if (curr_cmd->ElemCount != 0 && curr_cmd->TexRef != _CmdHeader.TexRef)
619 {
620 AddDrawCmd();
621 return;
622 }
623
624 // Unlike other _OnChangedXXX functions this may be called by ImFontAtlasUpdateDrawListsTextures() in more locations so we need to handle this case.
625 if (curr_cmd->UserCallback != NULL)
626 return;
627
628 // Try to merge with previous command if it matches, else use current command
629 ImDrawCmd* prev_cmd = curr_cmd - 1;
630 if (curr_cmd->ElemCount == 0 && CmdBuffer.Size > 1 && ImDrawCmd_HeaderCompare(&_CmdHeader, prev_cmd) == 0 && ImDrawCmd_AreSequentialIdxOffset(prev_cmd, curr_cmd) && prev_cmd->UserCallback == NULL)
631 {
632 CmdBuffer.pop_back();
633 return;
634 }
635 curr_cmd->TexRef = _CmdHeader.TexRef;
636}
637
638void ImDrawList::_OnChangedVtxOffset()
639{
640 // We don't need to compare curr_cmd->VtxOffset != _CmdHeader.VtxOffset because we know it'll be different at the time we call this.
641 _VtxCurrentIdx = 0;
642 IM_ASSERT_PARANOID(CmdBuffer.Size > 0);
643 ImDrawCmd* curr_cmd = &CmdBuffer.Data[CmdBuffer.Size - 1];
644 //IM_ASSERT(curr_cmd->VtxOffset != _CmdHeader.VtxOffset); // See #3349
645 if (curr_cmd->ElemCount != 0)
646 {
647 AddDrawCmd();
648 return;
649 }
650 IM_ASSERT(curr_cmd->UserCallback == NULL);
651 curr_cmd->VtxOffset = _CmdHeader.VtxOffset;
652}
653
654int ImDrawList::_CalcCircleAutoSegmentCount(float radius) const
655{
656 // Automatic segment count
657 const int radius_idx = (int)(radius + 0.999999f); // ceil to never reduce accuracy
658 if (radius_idx >= 0 && radius_idx < IM_COUNTOF(_Data->CircleSegmentCounts))
659 return _Data->CircleSegmentCounts[radius_idx]; // Use cached value
660 else
661 return IM_DRAWLIST_CIRCLE_AUTO_SEGMENT_CALC(radius, _Data->CircleSegmentMaxError);
662}
663
664// Render-level scissoring. This is passed down to your render function but not used for CPU-side coarse clipping. Prefer using higher-level ImGui::PushClipRect() to affect logic (hit-testing and widget culling)
665void ImDrawList::PushClipRect(const ImVec2& cr_min, const ImVec2& cr_max, bool intersect_with_current_clip_rect)
666{
667 ImVec4 cr(cr_min.x, cr_min.y, cr_max.x, cr_max.y);
668 if (intersect_with_current_clip_rect)
669 {
670 ImVec4 current = _CmdHeader.ClipRect;
671 if (cr.x < current.x) cr.x = current.x;
672 if (cr.y < current.y) cr.y = current.y;
673 if (cr.z > current.z) cr.z = current.z;
674 if (cr.w > current.w) cr.w = current.w;
675 }
676 cr.z = ImMax(cr.x, cr.z);
677 cr.w = ImMax(cr.y, cr.w);
678
679 _ClipRectStack.push_back(cr);
680 _CmdHeader.ClipRect = cr;
681 _OnChangedClipRect();
682}
683
684void ImDrawList::PushClipRectFullScreen()
685{
686 PushClipRect(ImVec2(_Data->ClipRectFullscreen.x, _Data->ClipRectFullscreen.y), ImVec2(_Data->ClipRectFullscreen.z, _Data->ClipRectFullscreen.w));
687}
688
689void ImDrawList::PopClipRect()
690{
691 _ClipRectStack.pop_back();
692 _CmdHeader.ClipRect = (_ClipRectStack.Size == 0) ? _Data->ClipRectFullscreen : _ClipRectStack.Data[_ClipRectStack.Size - 1];
693 _OnChangedClipRect();
694}
695
696void ImDrawList::PushTexture(ImTextureRef tex_ref)
697{
698 _TextureStack.push_back(tex_ref);
699 _CmdHeader.TexRef = tex_ref;
700 if (tex_ref._TexData != NULL)
701 IM_ASSERT(tex_ref._TexData->WantDestroyNextFrame == false);
702 _OnChangedTexture();
703}
704
705void ImDrawList::PopTexture()
706{
707 _TextureStack.pop_back();
708 _CmdHeader.TexRef = (_TextureStack.Size == 0) ? ImTextureRef() : _TextureStack.Data[_TextureStack.Size - 1];
709 _OnChangedTexture();
710}
711
712// This is used by ImGui::PushFont()/PopFont(). It works because we never use _TextureIdStack[] elsewhere than in PushTexture()/PopTexture().
713void ImDrawList::_SetTexture(ImTextureRef tex_ref)
714{
715 if (_CmdHeader.TexRef == tex_ref)
716 return;
717 _CmdHeader.TexRef = tex_ref;
718 _TextureStack.back() = tex_ref;
719 _OnChangedTexture();
720}
721
722// Reserve space for a number of vertices and indices.
723// You must finish filling your reserved data before calling PrimReserve() again, as it may reallocate or
724// submit the intermediate results. PrimUnreserve() can be used to release unused allocations.
725void ImDrawList::PrimReserve(int idx_count, int vtx_count)
726{
727 // Large mesh support (when enabled)
728 IM_ASSERT_PARANOID(idx_count >= 0 && vtx_count >= 0);
729 if (sizeof(ImDrawIdx) == 2 && (_VtxCurrentIdx + vtx_count >= (1 << 16)) && (Flags & ImDrawListFlags_AllowVtxOffset))
730 {
731 // FIXME: In theory we should be testing that vtx_count <64k here.
732 // In practice, RenderText() relies on reserving ahead for a worst case scenario so it is currently useful for us
733 // to not make that check until we rework the text functions to handle clipping and large horizontal lines better.
734 _CmdHeader.VtxOffset = VtxBuffer.Size;
735 _OnChangedVtxOffset();
736 }
737
738 ImDrawCmd* draw_cmd = &CmdBuffer.Data[CmdBuffer.Size - 1];
739 draw_cmd->ElemCount += idx_count;
740
741 int vtx_buffer_old_size = VtxBuffer.Size;
742 VtxBuffer.resize(vtx_buffer_old_size + vtx_count);
743 _VtxWritePtr = VtxBuffer.Data + vtx_buffer_old_size;
744
745 int idx_buffer_old_size = IdxBuffer.Size;
746 IdxBuffer.resize(idx_buffer_old_size + idx_count);
747 _IdxWritePtr = IdxBuffer.Data + idx_buffer_old_size;
748}
749
750// Release the number of reserved vertices/indices from the end of the last reservation made with PrimReserve().
751void ImDrawList::PrimUnreserve(int idx_count, int vtx_count)
752{
753 IM_ASSERT_PARANOID(idx_count >= 0 && vtx_count >= 0);
754
755 ImDrawCmd* draw_cmd = &CmdBuffer.Data[CmdBuffer.Size - 1];
756 draw_cmd->ElemCount -= idx_count;
757 VtxBuffer.shrink(VtxBuffer.Size - vtx_count);
758 IdxBuffer.shrink(IdxBuffer.Size - idx_count);
759}
760
761// Fully unrolled with inline call to keep our debug builds decently fast.
762void ImDrawList::PrimRect(const ImVec2& a, const ImVec2& c, ImU32 col)
763{
764 ImVec2 b(c.x, a.y), d(a.x, c.y), uv(_Data->TexUvWhitePixel);
765 ImDrawIdx idx = (ImDrawIdx)_VtxCurrentIdx;
766 _IdxWritePtr[0] = idx; _IdxWritePtr[1] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx+1); _IdxWritePtr[2] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx+2);
767 _IdxWritePtr[3] = idx; _IdxWritePtr[4] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx+2); _IdxWritePtr[5] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx+3);
768 _VtxWritePtr[0].pos = a; _VtxWritePtr[0].uv = uv; _VtxWritePtr[0].col = col;
769 _VtxWritePtr[1].pos = b; _VtxWritePtr[1].uv = uv; _VtxWritePtr[1].col = col;
770 _VtxWritePtr[2].pos = c; _VtxWritePtr[2].uv = uv; _VtxWritePtr[2].col = col;
771 _VtxWritePtr[3].pos = d; _VtxWritePtr[3].uv = uv; _VtxWritePtr[3].col = col;
772 _VtxWritePtr += 4;
773 _VtxCurrentIdx += 4;
774 _IdxWritePtr += 6;
775}
776
777void ImDrawList::PrimRectUV(const ImVec2& a, const ImVec2& c, const ImVec2& uv_a, const ImVec2& uv_c, ImU32 col)
778{
779 ImVec2 b(c.x, a.y), d(a.x, c.y), uv_b(uv_c.x, uv_a.y), uv_d(uv_a.x, uv_c.y);
780 ImDrawIdx idx = (ImDrawIdx)_VtxCurrentIdx;
781 _IdxWritePtr[0] = idx; _IdxWritePtr[1] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx+1); _IdxWritePtr[2] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx+2);
782 _IdxWritePtr[3] = idx; _IdxWritePtr[4] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx+2); _IdxWritePtr[5] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx+3);
783 _VtxWritePtr[0].pos = a; _VtxWritePtr[0].uv = uv_a; _VtxWritePtr[0].col = col;
784 _VtxWritePtr[1].pos = b; _VtxWritePtr[1].uv = uv_b; _VtxWritePtr[1].col = col;
785 _VtxWritePtr[2].pos = c; _VtxWritePtr[2].uv = uv_c; _VtxWritePtr[2].col = col;
786 _VtxWritePtr[3].pos = d; _VtxWritePtr[3].uv = uv_d; _VtxWritePtr[3].col = col;
787 _VtxWritePtr += 4;
788 _VtxCurrentIdx += 4;
789 _IdxWritePtr += 6;
790}
791
792void ImDrawList::PrimQuadUV(const ImVec2& a, const ImVec2& b, const ImVec2& c, const ImVec2& d, const ImVec2& uv_a, const ImVec2& uv_b, const ImVec2& uv_c, const ImVec2& uv_d, ImU32 col)
793{
794 ImDrawIdx idx = (ImDrawIdx)_VtxCurrentIdx;
795 _IdxWritePtr[0] = idx; _IdxWritePtr[1] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx+1); _IdxWritePtr[2] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx+2);
796 _IdxWritePtr[3] = idx; _IdxWritePtr[4] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx+2); _IdxWritePtr[5] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx+3);
797 _VtxWritePtr[0].pos = a; _VtxWritePtr[0].uv = uv_a; _VtxWritePtr[0].col = col;
798 _VtxWritePtr[1].pos = b; _VtxWritePtr[1].uv = uv_b; _VtxWritePtr[1].col = col;
799 _VtxWritePtr[2].pos = c; _VtxWritePtr[2].uv = uv_c; _VtxWritePtr[2].col = col;
800 _VtxWritePtr[3].pos = d; _VtxWritePtr[3].uv = uv_d; _VtxWritePtr[3].col = col;
801 _VtxWritePtr += 4;
802 _VtxCurrentIdx += 4;
803 _IdxWritePtr += 6;
804}
805
806// On AddPolyline() and AddConvexPolyFilled() we intentionally avoid using ImVec2 and superfluous function calls to optimize debug/non-inlined builds.
807// - Those macros expects l-values and need to be used as their own statement.
808// - Those macros are intentionally not surrounded by the 'do {} while (0)' idiom because even that translates to runtime with debug compilers.
809#define IM_NORMALIZE2F_OVER_ZERO(VX,VY) { float d2 = VX*VX + VY*VY; if (d2 > 0.0f) { float inv_len = ImRsqrt(d2); VX *= inv_len; VY *= inv_len; } } (void)0
810#define IM_FIXNORMAL2F_MAX_INVLEN2 100.0f // 500.0f (see #4053, #3366)
811#define IM_FIXNORMAL2F(VX,VY) { float d2 = VX*VX + VY*VY; if (d2 > 0.000001f) { float inv_len2 = 1.0f / d2; if (inv_len2 > IM_FIXNORMAL2F_MAX_INVLEN2) inv_len2 = IM_FIXNORMAL2F_MAX_INVLEN2; VX *= inv_len2; VY *= inv_len2; } } (void)0
812
813// TODO: Thickness anti-aliased lines cap are missing their AA fringe.
814// We avoid using the ImVec2 math operators here to reduce cost to a minimum for debug/non-inlined builds.
815void ImDrawList::AddPolyline(const ImVec2* points, const int points_count, ImU32 col, ImDrawFlags flags, float thickness)
816{
817 if (points_count < 2 || (col & IM_COL32_A_MASK) == 0)
818 return;
819
820 const bool closed = (flags & ImDrawFlags_Closed) != 0;
821 const ImVec2 opaque_uv = _Data->TexUvWhitePixel;
822 const int count = closed ? points_count : points_count - 1; // The number of line segments we need to draw
823 const bool thick_line = (thickness > _FringeScale);
824
825 if (Flags & ImDrawListFlags_AntiAliasedLines)
826 {
827 // Anti-aliased stroke
828 const float AA_SIZE = _FringeScale;
829 const ImU32 col_trans = col & ~IM_COL32_A_MASK;
830
831 // Thicknesses <1.0 should behave like thickness 1.0
832 thickness = ImMax(thickness, 1.0f);
833 const int integer_thickness = (int)thickness;
834 const float fractional_thickness = thickness - integer_thickness;
835
836 // Do we want to draw this line using a texture?
837 // - For now, only draw integer-width lines using textures to avoid issues with the way scaling occurs, could be improved.
838 // - If AA_SIZE is not 1.0f we cannot use the texture path.
839 const bool use_texture = (Flags & ImDrawListFlags_AntiAliasedLinesUseTex) && (integer_thickness < IM_DRAWLIST_TEX_LINES_WIDTH_MAX) && (fractional_thickness <= 0.00001f) && (AA_SIZE == 1.0f);
840
841 // We should never hit this, because NewFrame() doesn't set ImDrawListFlags_AntiAliasedLinesUseTex unless ImFontAtlasFlags_NoBakedLines is off
842 IM_ASSERT_PARANOID(!use_texture || !(_Data->Font->OwnerAtlas->Flags & ImFontAtlasFlags_NoBakedLines));
843
844 const int idx_count = use_texture ? (count * 6) : (thick_line ? count * 18 : count * 12);
845 const int vtx_count = use_texture ? (points_count * 2) : (thick_line ? points_count * 4 : points_count * 3);
846 PrimReserve(idx_count, vtx_count);
847
848 // Temporary buffer
849 // The first <points_count> items are normals at each line point, then after that there are either 2 or 4 temp points for each line point
850 _Data->TempBuffer.reserve_discard(points_count * ((use_texture || !thick_line) ? 3 : 5));
851 ImVec2* temp_normals = _Data->TempBuffer.Data;
852 ImVec2* temp_points = temp_normals + points_count;
853
854 // Calculate normals (tangents) for each line segment
855 for (int i1 = 0; i1 < count; i1++)
856 {
857 const int i2 = (i1 + 1) == points_count ? 0 : i1 + 1;
858 float dx = points[i2].x - points[i1].x;
859 float dy = points[i2].y - points[i1].y;
860 IM_NORMALIZE2F_OVER_ZERO(dx, dy);
861 temp_normals[i1].x = dy;
862 temp_normals[i1].y = -dx;
863 }
864 if (!closed)
865 temp_normals[points_count - 1] = temp_normals[points_count - 2];
866
867 // If we are drawing a one-pixel-wide line without a texture, or a textured line of any width, we only need 2 or 3 vertices per point
868 if (use_texture || !thick_line)
869 {
870 // [PATH 1] Texture-based lines (thick or non-thick)
871 // [PATH 2] Non texture-based lines (non-thick)
872
873 // The width of the geometry we need to draw - this is essentially <thickness> pixels for the line itself, plus "one pixel" for AA.
874 // - In the texture-based path, we don't use AA_SIZE here because the +1 is tied to the generated texture
875 // (see ImFontAtlasBuildRenderLinesTexData() function), and so alternate values won't work without changes to that code.
876 // - In the non texture-based paths, we would allow AA_SIZE to potentially be != 1.0f with a patch (e.g. fringe_scale patch to
877 // allow scaling geometry while preserving one-screen-pixel AA fringe).
878 const float half_draw_size = use_texture ? ((thickness * 0.5f) + 1) : AA_SIZE;
879
880 // If line is not closed, the first and last points need to be generated differently as there are no normals to blend
881 if (!closed)
882 {
883 temp_points[0] = points[0] + temp_normals[0] * half_draw_size;
884 temp_points[1] = points[0] - temp_normals[0] * half_draw_size;
885 temp_points[(points_count-1)*2+0] = points[points_count-1] + temp_normals[points_count-1] * half_draw_size;
886 temp_points[(points_count-1)*2+1] = points[points_count-1] - temp_normals[points_count-1] * half_draw_size;
887 }
888
889 // Generate the indices to form a number of triangles for each line segment, and the vertices for the line edges
890 // This takes points n and n+1 and writes into n+1, with the first point in a closed line being generated from the final one (as n+1 wraps)
891 // FIXME-OPT: Merge the different loops, possibly remove the temporary buffer.
892 unsigned int idx1 = _VtxCurrentIdx; // Vertex index for start of line segment
893 for (int i1 = 0; i1 < count; i1++) // i1 is the first point of the line segment
894 {
895 const int i2 = (i1 + 1) == points_count ? 0 : i1 + 1; // i2 is the second point of the line segment
896 const unsigned int idx2 = ((i1 + 1) == points_count) ? _VtxCurrentIdx : (idx1 + (use_texture ? 2 : 3)); // Vertex index for end of segment
897
898 // Average normals
899 float dm_x = (temp_normals[i1].x + temp_normals[i2].x) * 0.5f;
900 float dm_y = (temp_normals[i1].y + temp_normals[i2].y) * 0.5f;
901 IM_FIXNORMAL2F(dm_x, dm_y);
902 dm_x *= half_draw_size; // dm_x, dm_y are offset to the outer edge of the AA area
903 dm_y *= half_draw_size;
904
905 // Add temporary vertices for the outer edges
906 ImVec2* out_vtx = &temp_points[i2 * 2];
907 out_vtx[0].x = points[i2].x + dm_x;
908 out_vtx[0].y = points[i2].y + dm_y;
909 out_vtx[1].x = points[i2].x - dm_x;
910 out_vtx[1].y = points[i2].y - dm_y;
911
912 if (use_texture)
913 {
914 // Add indices for two triangles
915 _IdxWritePtr[0] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx2 + 0); _IdxWritePtr[1] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx1 + 0); _IdxWritePtr[2] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx1 + 1); // Right tri
916 _IdxWritePtr[3] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx2 + 1); _IdxWritePtr[4] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx1 + 1); _IdxWritePtr[5] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx2 + 0); // Left tri
917 _IdxWritePtr += 6;
918 }
919 else
920 {
921 // Add indexes for four triangles
922 _IdxWritePtr[0] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx2 + 0); _IdxWritePtr[1] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx1 + 0); _IdxWritePtr[2] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx1 + 2); // Right tri 1
923 _IdxWritePtr[3] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx1 + 2); _IdxWritePtr[4] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx2 + 2); _IdxWritePtr[5] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx2 + 0); // Right tri 2
924 _IdxWritePtr[6] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx2 + 1); _IdxWritePtr[7] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx1 + 1); _IdxWritePtr[8] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx1 + 0); // Left tri 1
925 _IdxWritePtr[9] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx1 + 0); _IdxWritePtr[10] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx2 + 0); _IdxWritePtr[11] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx2 + 1); // Left tri 2
926 _IdxWritePtr += 12;
927 }
928
929 idx1 = idx2;
930 }
931
932 // Add vertices for each point on the line
933 if (use_texture)
934 {
935 // If we're using textures we only need to emit the left/right edge vertices
936 ImVec4 tex_uvs = _Data->TexUvLines[integer_thickness];
937 /*if (fractional_thickness != 0.0f) // Currently always zero when use_texture==false!
938 {
939 const ImVec4 tex_uvs_1 = _Data->TexUvLines[integer_thickness + 1];
940 tex_uvs.x = tex_uvs.x + (tex_uvs_1.x - tex_uvs.x) * fractional_thickness; // inlined ImLerp()
941 tex_uvs.y = tex_uvs.y + (tex_uvs_1.y - tex_uvs.y) * fractional_thickness;
942 tex_uvs.z = tex_uvs.z + (tex_uvs_1.z - tex_uvs.z) * fractional_thickness;
943 tex_uvs.w = tex_uvs.w + (tex_uvs_1.w - tex_uvs.w) * fractional_thickness;
944 }*/
945 ImVec2 tex_uv0(tex_uvs.x, tex_uvs.y);
946 ImVec2 tex_uv1(tex_uvs.z, tex_uvs.w);
947 for (int i = 0; i < points_count; i++)
948 {
949 _VtxWritePtr[0].pos = temp_points[i * 2 + 0]; _VtxWritePtr[0].uv = tex_uv0; _VtxWritePtr[0].col = col; // Left-side outer edge
950 _VtxWritePtr[1].pos = temp_points[i * 2 + 1]; _VtxWritePtr[1].uv = tex_uv1; _VtxWritePtr[1].col = col; // Right-side outer edge
951 _VtxWritePtr += 2;
952 }
953 }
954 else
955 {
956 // If we're not using a texture, we need the center vertex as well
957 for (int i = 0; i < points_count; i++)
958 {
959 _VtxWritePtr[0].pos = points[i]; _VtxWritePtr[0].uv = opaque_uv; _VtxWritePtr[0].col = col; // Center of line
960 _VtxWritePtr[1].pos = temp_points[i * 2 + 0]; _VtxWritePtr[1].uv = opaque_uv; _VtxWritePtr[1].col = col_trans; // Left-side outer edge
961 _VtxWritePtr[2].pos = temp_points[i * 2 + 1]; _VtxWritePtr[2].uv = opaque_uv; _VtxWritePtr[2].col = col_trans; // Right-side outer edge
962 _VtxWritePtr += 3;
963 }
964 }
965 }
966 else
967 {
968 // [PATH 2] Non texture-based lines (thick): we need to draw the solid line core and thus require four vertices per point
969 const float half_inner_thickness = (thickness - AA_SIZE) * 0.5f;
970
971 // If line is not closed, the first and last points need to be generated differently as there are no normals to blend
972 if (!closed)
973 {
974 const int points_last = points_count - 1;
975 temp_points[0] = points[0] + temp_normals[0] * (half_inner_thickness + AA_SIZE);
976 temp_points[1] = points[0] + temp_normals[0] * (half_inner_thickness);
977 temp_points[2] = points[0] - temp_normals[0] * (half_inner_thickness);
978 temp_points[3] = points[0] - temp_normals[0] * (half_inner_thickness + AA_SIZE);
979 temp_points[points_last * 4 + 0] = points[points_last] + temp_normals[points_last] * (half_inner_thickness + AA_SIZE);
980 temp_points[points_last * 4 + 1] = points[points_last] + temp_normals[points_last] * (half_inner_thickness);
981 temp_points[points_last * 4 + 2] = points[points_last] - temp_normals[points_last] * (half_inner_thickness);
982 temp_points[points_last * 4 + 3] = points[points_last] - temp_normals[points_last] * (half_inner_thickness + AA_SIZE);
983 }
984
985 // Generate the indices to form a number of triangles for each line segment, and the vertices for the line edges
986 // This takes points n and n+1 and writes into n+1, with the first point in a closed line being generated from the final one (as n+1 wraps)
987 // FIXME-OPT: Merge the different loops, possibly remove the temporary buffer.
988 unsigned int idx1 = _VtxCurrentIdx; // Vertex index for start of line segment
989 for (int i1 = 0; i1 < count; i1++) // i1 is the first point of the line segment
990 {
991 const int i2 = (i1 + 1) == points_count ? 0 : (i1 + 1); // i2 is the second point of the line segment
992 const unsigned int idx2 = (i1 + 1) == points_count ? _VtxCurrentIdx : (idx1 + 4); // Vertex index for end of segment
993
994 // Average normals
995 float dm_x = (temp_normals[i1].x + temp_normals[i2].x) * 0.5f;
996 float dm_y = (temp_normals[i1].y + temp_normals[i2].y) * 0.5f;
997 IM_FIXNORMAL2F(dm_x, dm_y);
998 float dm_out_x = dm_x * (half_inner_thickness + AA_SIZE);
999 float dm_out_y = dm_y * (half_inner_thickness + AA_SIZE);
1000 float dm_in_x = dm_x * half_inner_thickness;
1001 float dm_in_y = dm_y * half_inner_thickness;
1002
1003 // Add temporary vertices
1004 ImVec2* out_vtx = &temp_points[i2 * 4];
1005 out_vtx[0].x = points[i2].x + dm_out_x;
1006 out_vtx[0].y = points[i2].y + dm_out_y;
1007 out_vtx[1].x = points[i2].x + dm_in_x;
1008 out_vtx[1].y = points[i2].y + dm_in_y;
1009 out_vtx[2].x = points[i2].x - dm_in_x;
1010 out_vtx[2].y = points[i2].y - dm_in_y;
1011 out_vtx[3].x = points[i2].x - dm_out_x;
1012 out_vtx[3].y = points[i2].y - dm_out_y;
1013
1014 // Add indexes
1015 _IdxWritePtr[0] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx2 + 1); _IdxWritePtr[1] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx1 + 1); _IdxWritePtr[2] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx1 + 2);
1016 _IdxWritePtr[3] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx1 + 2); _IdxWritePtr[4] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx2 + 2); _IdxWritePtr[5] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx2 + 1);
1017 _IdxWritePtr[6] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx2 + 1); _IdxWritePtr[7] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx1 + 1); _IdxWritePtr[8] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx1 + 0);
1018 _IdxWritePtr[9] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx1 + 0); _IdxWritePtr[10] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx2 + 0); _IdxWritePtr[11] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx2 + 1);
1019 _IdxWritePtr[12] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx2 + 2); _IdxWritePtr[13] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx1 + 2); _IdxWritePtr[14] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx1 + 3);
1020 _IdxWritePtr[15] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx1 + 3); _IdxWritePtr[16] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx2 + 3); _IdxWritePtr[17] = (ImDrawIdx)(idx2 + 2);
1021 _IdxWritePtr += 18;
1022
1023 idx1 = idx2;
1024 }
1025
1026 // Add vertices
1027 for (int i = 0; i < points_count; i++)
1028 {
1029 _VtxWritePtr[0].pos = temp_points[i * 4 + 0]; _VtxWritePtr[0].uv = opaque_uv; _VtxWritePtr[0].col = col_trans;
1030 _VtxWritePtr[1].pos = temp_points[i * 4 + 1]; _VtxWritePtr[1].uv = opaque_uv; _VtxWritePtr[1].col = col;
1031 _VtxWritePtr[2].pos = temp_points[i * 4 + 2]; _VtxWritePtr[2].uv = opaque_uv; _VtxWritePtr[2].col = col;
1032 _VtxWritePtr[3].pos = temp_points[i * 4 + 3]; _VtxWritePtr[3].uv = opaque_uv; _VtxWritePtr[3].col = col_trans;
1033 _VtxWritePtr += 4;
1034 }
1035 }
1036 _VtxCurrentIdx += (ImDrawIdx)vtx_count;
1037 }
1038 else
1039 {
1040 // [PATH 4] Non texture-based, Non anti-aliased lines
1041 const int idx_count = count * 6;
1042 const int vtx_count = count * 4; // FIXME-OPT: Not sharing edges
1043 PrimReserve(idx_count, vtx_count);
1044
1045 for (int i1 = 0; i1 < count; i1++)
1046 {
1047 const int i2 = (i1 + 1) == points_count ? 0 : i1 + 1;
1048 const ImVec2& p1 = points[i1];
1049 const ImVec2& p2 = points[i2];
1050
1051 float dx = p2.x - p1.x;
1052 float dy = p2.y - p1.y;
1053 IM_NORMALIZE2F_OVER_ZERO(dx, dy);
1054 dx *= (thickness * 0.5f);
1055 dy *= (thickness * 0.5f);
1056
1057 _VtxWritePtr[0].pos.x = p1.x + dy; _VtxWritePtr[0].pos.y = p1.y - dx; _VtxWritePtr[0].uv = opaque_uv; _VtxWritePtr[0].col = col;
1058 _VtxWritePtr[1].pos.x = p2.x + dy; _VtxWritePtr[1].pos.y = p2.y - dx; _VtxWritePtr[1].uv = opaque_uv; _VtxWritePtr[1].col = col;
1059 _VtxWritePtr[2].pos.x = p2.x - dy; _VtxWritePtr[2].pos.y = p2.y + dx; _VtxWritePtr[2].uv = opaque_uv; _VtxWritePtr[2].col = col;
1060 _VtxWritePtr[3].pos.x = p1.x - dy; _VtxWritePtr[3].pos.y = p1.y + dx; _VtxWritePtr[3].uv = opaque_uv; _VtxWritePtr[3].col = col;
1061 _VtxWritePtr += 4;
1062
1063 _IdxWritePtr[0] = (ImDrawIdx)(_VtxCurrentIdx); _IdxWritePtr[1] = (ImDrawIdx)(_VtxCurrentIdx + 1); _IdxWritePtr[2] = (ImDrawIdx)(_VtxCurrentIdx + 2);
1064 _IdxWritePtr[3] = (ImDrawIdx)(_VtxCurrentIdx); _IdxWritePtr[4] = (ImDrawIdx)(_VtxCurrentIdx + 2); _IdxWritePtr[5] = (ImDrawIdx)(_VtxCurrentIdx + 3);
1065 _IdxWritePtr += 6;
1066 _VtxCurrentIdx += 4;
1067 }
1068 }
1069}
1070
1071// - We intentionally avoid using ImVec2 and its math operators here to reduce cost to a minimum for debug/non-inlined builds.
1072// - Filled shapes must always use clockwise winding order. The anti-aliasing fringe depends on it. Counter-clockwise shapes will have "inward" anti-aliasing.
1073void ImDrawList::AddConvexPolyFilled(const ImVec2* points, const int points_count, ImU32 col)
1074{
1075 if (points_count < 3 || (col & IM_COL32_A_MASK) == 0)
1076 return;
1077
1078 const ImVec2 uv = _Data->TexUvWhitePixel;
1079
1080 if (Flags & ImDrawListFlags_AntiAliasedFill)
1081 {
1082 // Anti-aliased Fill
1083 const float AA_SIZE = _FringeScale;
1084 const ImU32 col_trans = col & ~IM_COL32_A_MASK;
1085 const int idx_count = (points_count - 2)*3 + points_count * 6;
1086 const int vtx_count = (points_count * 2);
1087 PrimReserve(idx_count, vtx_count);
1088
1089 // Add indexes for fill
1090 unsigned int vtx_inner_idx = _VtxCurrentIdx;
1091 unsigned int vtx_outer_idx = _VtxCurrentIdx + 1;
1092 for (int i = 2; i < points_count; i++)
1093 {
1094 _IdxWritePtr[0] = (ImDrawIdx)(vtx_inner_idx); _IdxWritePtr[1] = (ImDrawIdx)(vtx_inner_idx + ((i - 1) << 1)); _IdxWritePtr[2] = (ImDrawIdx)(vtx_inner_idx + (i << 1));
1095 _IdxWritePtr += 3;
1096 }
1097
1098 // Compute normals
1099 _Data->TempBuffer.reserve_discard(points_count);
1100 ImVec2* temp_normals = _Data->TempBuffer.Data;
1101 for (int i0 = points_count - 1, i1 = 0; i1 < points_count; i0 = i1++)
1102 {
1103 const ImVec2& p0 = points[i0];
1104 const ImVec2& p1 = points[i1];
1105 float dx = p1.x - p0.x;
1106 float dy = p1.y - p0.y;
1107 IM_NORMALIZE2F_OVER_ZERO(dx, dy);
1108 temp_normals[i0].x = dy;
1109 temp_normals[i0].y = -dx;
1110 }
1111
1112 for (int i0 = points_count - 1, i1 = 0; i1 < points_count; i0 = i1++)
1113 {
1114 // Average normals
1115 const ImVec2& n0 = temp_normals[i0];
1116 const ImVec2& n1 = temp_normals[i1];
1117 float dm_x = (n0.x + n1.x) * 0.5f;
1118 float dm_y = (n0.y + n1.y) * 0.5f;
1119 IM_FIXNORMAL2F(dm_x, dm_y);
1120 dm_x *= AA_SIZE * 0.5f;
1121 dm_y *= AA_SIZE * 0.5f;
1122
1123 // Add vertices
1124 _VtxWritePtr[0].pos.x = (points[i1].x - dm_x); _VtxWritePtr[0].pos.y = (points[i1].y - dm_y); _VtxWritePtr[0].uv = uv; _VtxWritePtr[0].col = col; // Inner
1125 _VtxWritePtr[1].pos.x = (points[i1].x + dm_x); _VtxWritePtr[1].pos.y = (points[i1].y + dm_y); _VtxWritePtr[1].uv = uv; _VtxWritePtr[1].col = col_trans; // Outer
1126 _VtxWritePtr += 2;
1127
1128 // Add indexes for fringes
1129 _IdxWritePtr[0] = (ImDrawIdx)(vtx_inner_idx + (i1 << 1)); _IdxWritePtr[1] = (ImDrawIdx)(vtx_inner_idx + (i0 << 1)); _IdxWritePtr[2] = (ImDrawIdx)(vtx_outer_idx + (i0 << 1));
1130 _IdxWritePtr[3] = (ImDrawIdx)(vtx_outer_idx + (i0 << 1)); _IdxWritePtr[4] = (ImDrawIdx)(vtx_outer_idx + (i1 << 1)); _IdxWritePtr[5] = (ImDrawIdx)(vtx_inner_idx + (i1 << 1));
1131 _IdxWritePtr += 6;
1132 }
1133 _VtxCurrentIdx += (ImDrawIdx)vtx_count;
1134 }
1135 else
1136 {
1137 // Non Anti-aliased Fill
1138 const int idx_count = (points_count - 2)*3;
1139 const int vtx_count = points_count;
1140 PrimReserve(idx_count, vtx_count);
1141 for (int i = 0; i < vtx_count; i++)
1142 {
1143 _VtxWritePtr[0].pos = points[i]; _VtxWritePtr[0].uv = uv; _VtxWritePtr[0].col = col;
1144 _VtxWritePtr++;
1145 }
1146 for (int i = 2; i < points_count; i++)
1147 {
1148 _IdxWritePtr[0] = (ImDrawIdx)(_VtxCurrentIdx); _IdxWritePtr[1] = (ImDrawIdx)(_VtxCurrentIdx + i - 1); _IdxWritePtr[2] = (ImDrawIdx)(_VtxCurrentIdx + i);
1149 _IdxWritePtr += 3;
1150 }
1151 _VtxCurrentIdx += (ImDrawIdx)vtx_count;
1152 }
1153}
1154
1155void ImDrawList::_PathArcToFastEx(const ImVec2& center, float radius, int a_min_sample, int a_max_sample, int a_step)
1156{
1157 if (radius < 0.5f)
1158 {
1159 _Path.push_back(center);
1160 return;
1161 }
1162
1163 // Calculate arc auto segment step size
1164 if (a_step <= 0)
1165 a_step = IM_DRAWLIST_ARCFAST_SAMPLE_MAX / _CalcCircleAutoSegmentCount(radius);
1166
1167 // Make sure we never do steps larger than one quarter of the circle
1168 a_step = ImClamp(a_step, 1, IM_DRAWLIST_ARCFAST_TABLE_SIZE / 4);
1169
1170 const int sample_range = ImAbs(a_max_sample - a_min_sample);
1171 const int a_next_step = a_step;
1172
1173 int samples = sample_range + 1;
1174 bool extra_max_sample = false;
1175 if (a_step > 1)
1176 {
1177 samples = sample_range / a_step + 1;
1178 const int overstep = sample_range % a_step;
1179
1180 if (overstep > 0)
1181 {
1182 extra_max_sample = true;
1183 samples++;
1184
1185 // When we have overstep to avoid awkwardly looking one long line and one tiny one at the end,
1186 // distribute first step range evenly between them by reducing first step size.
1187 if (sample_range > 0)
1188 a_step -= (a_step - overstep) / 2;
1189 }
1190 }
1191
1192 _Path.resize(_Path.Size + samples);
1193 ImVec2* out_ptr = _Path.Data + (_Path.Size - samples);
1194
1195 int sample_index = a_min_sample;
1196 if (sample_index < 0 || sample_index >= IM_DRAWLIST_ARCFAST_SAMPLE_MAX)
1197 {
1198 sample_index = sample_index % IM_DRAWLIST_ARCFAST_SAMPLE_MAX;
1199 if (sample_index < 0)
1200 sample_index += IM_DRAWLIST_ARCFAST_SAMPLE_MAX;
1201 }
1202
1203 if (a_max_sample >= a_min_sample)
1204 {
1205 for (int a = a_min_sample; a <= a_max_sample; a += a_step, sample_index += a_step, a_step = a_next_step)
1206 {
1207 // a_step is clamped to IM_DRAWLIST_ARCFAST_SAMPLE_MAX, so we have guaranteed that it will not wrap over range twice or more
1208 if (sample_index >= IM_DRAWLIST_ARCFAST_SAMPLE_MAX)
1209 sample_index -= IM_DRAWLIST_ARCFAST_SAMPLE_MAX;
1210
1211 const ImVec2 s = _Data->ArcFastVtx[sample_index];
1212 out_ptr->x = center.x + s.x * radius;
1213 out_ptr->y = center.y + s.y * radius;
1214 out_ptr++;
1215 }
1216 }
1217 else
1218 {
1219 for (int a = a_min_sample; a >= a_max_sample; a -= a_step, sample_index -= a_step, a_step = a_next_step)
1220 {
1221 // a_step is clamped to IM_DRAWLIST_ARCFAST_SAMPLE_MAX, so we have guaranteed that it will not wrap over range twice or more
1222 if (sample_index < 0)
1223 sample_index += IM_DRAWLIST_ARCFAST_SAMPLE_MAX;
1224
1225 const ImVec2 s = _Data->ArcFastVtx[sample_index];
1226 out_ptr->x = center.x + s.x * radius;
1227 out_ptr->y = center.y + s.y * radius;
1228 out_ptr++;
1229 }
1230 }
1231
1232 if (extra_max_sample)
1233 {
1234 int normalized_max_sample = a_max_sample % IM_DRAWLIST_ARCFAST_SAMPLE_MAX;
1235 if (normalized_max_sample < 0)
1236 normalized_max_sample += IM_DRAWLIST_ARCFAST_SAMPLE_MAX;
1237
1238 const ImVec2 s = _Data->ArcFastVtx[normalized_max_sample];
1239 out_ptr->x = center.x + s.x * radius;
1240 out_ptr->y = center.y + s.y * radius;
1241 out_ptr++;
1242 }
1243
1244 IM_ASSERT_PARANOID(_Path.Data + _Path.Size == out_ptr);
1245}
1246
1247void ImDrawList::_PathArcToN(const ImVec2& center, float radius, float a_min, float a_max, int num_segments)
1248{
1249 if (radius < 0.5f)
1250 {
1251 _Path.push_back(center);
1252 return;
1253 }
1254
1255 // Note that we are adding a point at both a_min and a_max.
1256 // If you are trying to draw a full closed circle you don't want the overlapping points!
1257 _Path.reserve(_Path.Size + (num_segments + 1));
1258 for (int i = 0; i <= num_segments; i++)
1259 {
1260 const float a = a_min + ((float)i / (float)num_segments) * (a_max - a_min);
1261 _Path.push_back(ImVec2(center.x + ImCos(a) * radius, center.y + ImSin(a) * radius));
1262 }
1263}
1264
1265// 0: East, 3: South, 6: West, 9: North, 12: East
1266void ImDrawList::PathArcToFast(const ImVec2& center, float radius, int a_min_of_12, int a_max_of_12)
1267{
1268 if (radius < 0.5f)
1269 {
1270 _Path.push_back(center);
1271 return;
1272 }
1273 _PathArcToFastEx(center, radius, a_min_of_12 * IM_DRAWLIST_ARCFAST_SAMPLE_MAX / 12, a_max_of_12 * IM_DRAWLIST_ARCFAST_SAMPLE_MAX / 12, 0);
1274}
1275
1276void ImDrawList::PathArcTo(const ImVec2& center, float radius, float a_min, float a_max, int num_segments)
1277{
1278 if (radius < 0.5f)
1279 {
1280 _Path.push_back(center);
1281 return;
1282 }
1283
1284 if (num_segments > 0)
1285 {
1286 _PathArcToN(center, radius, a_min, a_max, num_segments);
1287 return;
1288 }
1289
1290 // Automatic segment count
1291 if (radius <= _Data->ArcFastRadiusCutoff)
1292 {
1293 const bool a_is_reverse = a_max < a_min;
1294
1295 // We are going to use precomputed values for mid samples.
1296 // Determine first and last sample in lookup table that belong to the arc.
1297 const float a_min_sample_f = IM_DRAWLIST_ARCFAST_SAMPLE_MAX * a_min / (IM_PI * 2.0f);
1298 const float a_max_sample_f = IM_DRAWLIST_ARCFAST_SAMPLE_MAX * a_max / (IM_PI * 2.0f);
1299
1300 const int a_min_sample = a_is_reverse ? (int)ImFloor(a_min_sample_f) : (int)ImCeil(a_min_sample_f);
1301 const int a_max_sample = a_is_reverse ? (int)ImCeil(a_max_sample_f) : (int)ImFloor(a_max_sample_f);
1302 const int a_mid_samples = a_is_reverse ? ImMax(a_min_sample - a_max_sample, 0) : ImMax(a_max_sample - a_min_sample, 0);
1303
1304 const float a_min_segment_angle = a_min_sample * IM_PI * 2.0f / IM_DRAWLIST_ARCFAST_SAMPLE_MAX;
1305 const float a_max_segment_angle = a_max_sample * IM_PI * 2.0f / IM_DRAWLIST_ARCFAST_SAMPLE_MAX;
1306 const bool a_emit_start = ImAbs(a_min_segment_angle - a_min) >= 1e-5f;
1307 const bool a_emit_end = ImAbs(a_max - a_max_segment_angle) >= 1e-5f;
1308
1309 _Path.reserve(_Path.Size + (a_mid_samples + 1 + (a_emit_start ? 1 : 0) + (a_emit_end ? 1 : 0)));
1310 if (a_emit_start)
1311 _Path.push_back(ImVec2(center.x + ImCos(a_min) * radius, center.y + ImSin(a_min) * radius));
1312 if (a_mid_samples > 0)
1313 _PathArcToFastEx(center, radius, a_min_sample, a_max_sample, 0);
1314 if (a_emit_end)
1315 _Path.push_back(ImVec2(center.x + ImCos(a_max) * radius, center.y + ImSin(a_max) * radius));
1316 }
1317 else
1318 {
1319 const float arc_length = ImAbs(a_max - a_min);
1320 const int circle_segment_count = _CalcCircleAutoSegmentCount(radius);
1321 const int arc_segment_count = ImMax((int)ImCeil(circle_segment_count * arc_length / (IM_PI * 2.0f)), (int)(2.0f * IM_PI / arc_length));
1322 _PathArcToN(center, radius, a_min, a_max, arc_segment_count);
1323 }
1324}
1325
1326void ImDrawList::PathEllipticalArcTo(const ImVec2& center, const ImVec2& radius, float rot, float a_min, float a_max, int num_segments)
1327{
1328 if (num_segments <= 0)
1329 num_segments = _CalcCircleAutoSegmentCount(ImMax(radius.x, radius.y)); // A bit pessimistic, maybe there's a better computation to do here.
1330
1331 _Path.reserve(_Path.Size + (num_segments + 1));
1332
1333 const float cos_rot = ImCos(rot);
1334 const float sin_rot = ImSin(rot);
1335 for (int i = 0; i <= num_segments; i++)
1336 {
1337 const float a = a_min + ((float)i / (float)num_segments) * (a_max - a_min);
1338 ImVec2 point(ImCos(a) * radius.x, ImSin(a) * radius.y);
1339 const ImVec2 rel((point.x * cos_rot) - (point.y * sin_rot), (point.x * sin_rot) + (point.y * cos_rot));
1340 point.x = rel.x + center.x;
1341 point.y = rel.y + center.y;
1342 _Path.push_back(point);
1343 }
1344}
1345
1346ImVec2 ImBezierCubicCalc(const ImVec2& p1, const ImVec2& p2, const ImVec2& p3, const ImVec2& p4, float t)
1347{
1348 float u = 1.0f - t;
1349 float w1 = u * u * u;
1350 float w2 = 3 * u * u * t;
1351 float w3 = 3 * u * t * t;
1352 float w4 = t * t * t;
1353 return ImVec2(w1 * p1.x + w2 * p2.x + w3 * p3.x + w4 * p4.x, w1 * p1.y + w2 * p2.y + w3 * p3.y + w4 * p4.y);
1354}
1355
1356ImVec2 ImBezierQuadraticCalc(const ImVec2& p1, const ImVec2& p2, const ImVec2& p3, float t)
1357{
1358 float u = 1.0f - t;
1359 float w1 = u * u;
1360 float w2 = 2 * u * t;
1361 float w3 = t * t;
1362 return ImVec2(w1 * p1.x + w2 * p2.x + w3 * p3.x, w1 * p1.y + w2 * p2.y + w3 * p3.y);
1363}
1364
1365// Closely mimics ImBezierCubicClosestPointCasteljau() in imgui.cpp
1366static void PathBezierCubicCurveToCasteljau(ImVector<ImVec2>* path, float x1, float y1, float x2, float y2, float x3, float y3, float x4, float y4, float tess_tol, int level)
1367{
1368 float dx = x4 - x1;
1369 float dy = y4 - y1;
1370 float d2 = (x2 - x4) * dy - (y2 - y4) * dx;
1371 float d3 = (x3 - x4) * dy - (y3 - y4) * dx;
1372 d2 = (d2 >= 0) ? d2 : -d2;
1373 d3 = (d3 >= 0) ? d3 : -d3;
1374 if ((d2 + d3) * (d2 + d3) < tess_tol * (dx * dx + dy * dy))
1375 {
1376 path->push_back(ImVec2(x4, y4));
1377 }
1378 else if (level < 10)
1379 {
1380 float x12 = (x1 + x2) * 0.5f, y12 = (y1 + y2) * 0.5f;
1381 float x23 = (x2 + x3) * 0.5f, y23 = (y2 + y3) * 0.5f;
1382 float x34 = (x3 + x4) * 0.5f, y34 = (y3 + y4) * 0.5f;
1383 float x123 = (x12 + x23) * 0.5f, y123 = (y12 + y23) * 0.5f;
1384 float x234 = (x23 + x34) * 0.5f, y234 = (y23 + y34) * 0.5f;
1385 float x1234 = (x123 + x234) * 0.5f, y1234 = (y123 + y234) * 0.5f;
1386 PathBezierCubicCurveToCasteljau(path, x1, y1, x12, y12, x123, y123, x1234, y1234, tess_tol, level + 1);
1387 PathBezierCubicCurveToCasteljau(path, x1234, y1234, x234, y234, x34, y34, x4, y4, tess_tol, level + 1);
1388 }
1389}
1390
1391static void PathBezierQuadraticCurveToCasteljau(ImVector<ImVec2>* path, float x1, float y1, float x2, float y2, float x3, float y3, float tess_tol, int level)
1392{
1393 float dx = x3 - x1, dy = y3 - y1;
1394 float det = (x2 - x3) * dy - (y2 - y3) * dx;
1395 if (det * det * 4.0f < tess_tol * (dx * dx + dy * dy))
1396 {
1397 path->push_back(ImVec2(x3, y3));
1398 }
1399 else if (level < 10)
1400 {
1401 float x12 = (x1 + x2) * 0.5f, y12 = (y1 + y2) * 0.5f;
1402 float x23 = (x2 + x3) * 0.5f, y23 = (y2 + y3) * 0.5f;
1403 float x123 = (x12 + x23) * 0.5f, y123 = (y12 + y23) * 0.5f;
1404 PathBezierQuadraticCurveToCasteljau(path, x1, y1, x12, y12, x123, y123, tess_tol, level + 1);
1405 PathBezierQuadraticCurveToCasteljau(path, x123, y123, x23, y23, x3, y3, tess_tol, level + 1);
1406 }
1407}
1408
1409void ImDrawList::PathBezierCubicCurveTo(const ImVec2& p2, const ImVec2& p3, const ImVec2& p4, int num_segments)
1410{
1411 ImVec2 p1 = _Path.back();
1412 if (num_segments == 0)
1413 {
1414 IM_ASSERT(_Data->CurveTessellationTol > 0.0f);
1415 PathBezierCubicCurveToCasteljau(&_Path, p1.x, p1.y, p2.x, p2.y, p3.x, p3.y, p4.x, p4.y, _Data->CurveTessellationTol, 0); // Auto-tessellated
1416 }
1417 else
1418 {
1419 float t_step = 1.0f / (float)num_segments;
1420 for (int i_step = 1; i_step <= num_segments; i_step++)
1421 _Path.push_back(ImBezierCubicCalc(p1, p2, p3, p4, t_step * i_step));
1422 }
1423}
1424
1425void ImDrawList::PathBezierQuadraticCurveTo(const ImVec2& p2, const ImVec2& p3, int num_segments)
1426{
1427 ImVec2 p1 = _Path.back();
1428 if (num_segments == 0)
1429 {
1430 IM_ASSERT(_Data->CurveTessellationTol > 0.0f);
1431 PathBezierQuadraticCurveToCasteljau(&_Path, p1.x, p1.y, p2.x, p2.y, p3.x, p3.y, _Data->CurveTessellationTol, 0);// Auto-tessellated
1432 }
1433 else
1434 {
1435 float t_step = 1.0f / (float)num_segments;
1436 for (int i_step = 1; i_step <= num_segments; i_step++)
1437 _Path.push_back(ImBezierQuadraticCalc(p1, p2, p3, t_step * i_step));
1438 }
1439}
1440
1441static inline ImDrawFlags FixRectCornerFlags(ImDrawFlags flags)
1442{
1443 /*
1444 IM_STATIC_ASSERT(ImDrawFlags_RoundCornersTopLeft == (1 << 4));
1445#ifndef IMGUI_DISABLE_OBSOLETE_FUNCTIONS
1446 // Obsoleted in 1.82 (from February 2021). This code was stripped/simplified and mostly commented in 1.90 (from September 2023)
1447 // - Legacy Support for hard coded ~0 (used to be a suggested equivalent to ImDrawCornerFlags_All)
1448 if (flags == ~0) { return ImDrawFlags_RoundCornersAll; }
1449 // - Legacy Support for hard coded 0x01 to 0x0F (matching 15 out of 16 old flags combinations). Read details in older version of this code.
1450 if (flags >= 0x01 && flags <= 0x0F) { return (flags << 4); }
1451 // We cannot support hard coded 0x00 with 'float rounding > 0.0f' --> replace with ImDrawFlags_RoundCornersNone or use 'float rounding = 0.0f'
1452#endif
1453 */
1454 // If this assert triggers, please update your code replacing hardcoded values with new ImDrawFlags_RoundCorners* values.
1455 // Note that ImDrawFlags_Closed (== 0x01) is an invalid flag for AddRect(), AddRectFilled(), PathRect() etc. anyway.
1456 // See details in 1.82 Changelog as well as 2021/03/12 and 2023/09/08 entries in "API BREAKING CHANGES" section.
1457 IM_ASSERT((flags & 0x0F) == 0 && "Misuse of legacy hardcoded ImDrawCornerFlags values!");
1458
1459 if ((flags & ImDrawFlags_RoundCornersMask_) == 0)
1460 flags |= ImDrawFlags_RoundCornersAll;
1461
1462 return flags;
1463}
1464
1465void ImDrawList::PathRect(const ImVec2& a, const ImVec2& b, float rounding, ImDrawFlags flags)
1466{
1467 if (rounding >= 0.5f)
1468 {
1469 flags = FixRectCornerFlags(flags);
1470 rounding = ImMin(rounding, ImFabs(b.x - a.x) * (((flags & ImDrawFlags_RoundCornersTop) == ImDrawFlags_RoundCornersTop) || ((flags & ImDrawFlags_RoundCornersBottom) == ImDrawFlags_RoundCornersBottom) ? 0.5f : 1.0f) - 1.0f);
1471 rounding = ImMin(rounding, ImFabs(b.y - a.y) * (((flags & ImDrawFlags_RoundCornersLeft) == ImDrawFlags_RoundCornersLeft) || ((flags & ImDrawFlags_RoundCornersRight) == ImDrawFlags_RoundCornersRight) ? 0.5f : 1.0f) - 1.0f);
1472 }
1473 if (rounding < 0.5f || (flags & ImDrawFlags_RoundCornersMask_) == ImDrawFlags_RoundCornersNone)
1474 {
1475 PathLineTo(a);
1476 PathLineTo(ImVec2(b.x, a.y));
1477 PathLineTo(b);
1478 PathLineTo(ImVec2(a.x, b.y));
1479 }
1480 else
1481 {
1482 const float rounding_tl = (flags & ImDrawFlags_RoundCornersTopLeft) ? rounding : 0.0f;
1483 const float rounding_tr = (flags & ImDrawFlags_RoundCornersTopRight) ? rounding : 0.0f;
1484 const float rounding_br = (flags & ImDrawFlags_RoundCornersBottomRight) ? rounding : 0.0f;
1485 const float rounding_bl = (flags & ImDrawFlags_RoundCornersBottomLeft) ? rounding : 0.0f;
1486 PathArcToFast(ImVec2(a.x + rounding_tl, a.y + rounding_tl), rounding_tl, 6, 9);
1487 PathArcToFast(ImVec2(b.x - rounding_tr, a.y + rounding_tr), rounding_tr, 9, 12);
1488 PathArcToFast(ImVec2(b.x - rounding_br, b.y - rounding_br), rounding_br, 0, 3);
1489 PathArcToFast(ImVec2(a.x + rounding_bl, b.y - rounding_bl), rounding_bl, 3, 6);
1490 }
1491}
1492
1493void ImDrawList::AddLine(const ImVec2& p1, const ImVec2& p2, ImU32 col, float thickness)
1494{
1495 if ((col & IM_COL32_A_MASK) == 0)
1496 return;
1497 PathLineTo(p1 + ImVec2(0.5f, 0.5f));
1498 PathLineTo(p2 + ImVec2(0.5f, 0.5f));
1499 PathStroke(col, 0, thickness);
1500}
1501
1502// p_min = upper-left, p_max = lower-right
1503// Note we don't render 1 pixels sized rectangles properly.
1504void ImDrawList::AddRect(const ImVec2& p_min, const ImVec2& p_max, ImU32 col, float rounding, ImDrawFlags flags, float thickness)
1505{
1506 if ((col & IM_COL32_A_MASK) == 0)
1507 return;
1508 if (Flags & ImDrawListFlags_AntiAliasedLines)
1509 PathRect(p_min + ImVec2(0.50f, 0.50f), p_max - ImVec2(0.50f, 0.50f), rounding, flags);
1510 else
1511 PathRect(p_min + ImVec2(0.50f, 0.50f), p_max - ImVec2(0.49f, 0.49f), rounding, flags); // Better looking lower-right corner and rounded non-AA shapes.
1512 PathStroke(col, ImDrawFlags_Closed, thickness);
1513}
1514
1515void ImDrawList::AddRectFilled(const ImVec2& p_min, const ImVec2& p_max, ImU32 col, float rounding, ImDrawFlags flags)
1516{
1517 if ((col & IM_COL32_A_MASK) == 0)
1518 return;
1519 if (rounding < 0.5f || (flags & ImDrawFlags_RoundCornersMask_) == ImDrawFlags_RoundCornersNone)
1520 {
1521 PrimReserve(6, 4);
1522 PrimRect(p_min, p_max, col);
1523 }
1524 else
1525 {
1526 PathRect(p_min, p_max, rounding, flags);
1527 PathFillConvex(col);
1528 }
1529}
1530
1531// p_min = upper-left, p_max = lower-right
1532void ImDrawList::AddRectFilledMultiColor(const ImVec2& p_min, const ImVec2& p_max, ImU32 col_upr_left, ImU32 col_upr_right, ImU32 col_bot_right, ImU32 col_bot_left)
1533{
1534 if (((col_upr_left | col_upr_right | col_bot_right | col_bot_left) & IM_COL32_A_MASK) == 0)
1535 return;
1536
1537 const ImVec2 uv = _Data->TexUvWhitePixel;
1538 PrimReserve(6, 4);
1539 PrimWriteIdx((ImDrawIdx)(_VtxCurrentIdx)); PrimWriteIdx((ImDrawIdx)(_VtxCurrentIdx + 1)); PrimWriteIdx((ImDrawIdx)(_VtxCurrentIdx + 2));
1540 PrimWriteIdx((ImDrawIdx)(_VtxCurrentIdx)); PrimWriteIdx((ImDrawIdx)(_VtxCurrentIdx + 2)); PrimWriteIdx((ImDrawIdx)(_VtxCurrentIdx + 3));
1541 PrimWriteVtx(p_min, uv, col_upr_left);
1542 PrimWriteVtx(ImVec2(p_max.x, p_min.y), uv, col_upr_right);
1543 PrimWriteVtx(p_max, uv, col_bot_right);
1544 PrimWriteVtx(ImVec2(p_min.x, p_max.y), uv, col_bot_left);
1545}
1546
1547void ImDrawList::AddQuad(const ImVec2& p1, const ImVec2& p2, const ImVec2& p3, const ImVec2& p4, ImU32 col, float thickness)
1548{
1549 if ((col & IM_COL32_A_MASK) == 0)
1550 return;
1551
1552 PathLineTo(p1);
1553 PathLineTo(p2);
1554 PathLineTo(p3);
1555 PathLineTo(p4);
1556 PathStroke(col, ImDrawFlags_Closed, thickness);
1557}
1558
1559void ImDrawList::AddQuadFilled(const ImVec2& p1, const ImVec2& p2, const ImVec2& p3, const ImVec2& p4, ImU32 col)
1560{
1561 if ((col & IM_COL32_A_MASK) == 0)
1562 return;
1563
1564 PathLineTo(p1);
1565 PathLineTo(p2);
1566 PathLineTo(p3);
1567 PathLineTo(p4);
1568 PathFillConvex(col);
1569}
1570
1571void ImDrawList::AddTriangle(const ImVec2& p1, const ImVec2& p2, const ImVec2& p3, ImU32 col, float thickness)
1572{
1573 if ((col & IM_COL32_A_MASK) == 0)
1574 return;
1575
1576 PathLineTo(p1);
1577 PathLineTo(p2);
1578 PathLineTo(p3);
1579 PathStroke(col, ImDrawFlags_Closed, thickness);
1580}
1581
1582void ImDrawList::AddTriangleFilled(const ImVec2& p1, const ImVec2& p2, const ImVec2& p3, ImU32 col)
1583{
1584 if ((col & IM_COL32_A_MASK) == 0)
1585 return;
1586
1587 PathLineTo(p1);
1588 PathLineTo(p2);
1589 PathLineTo(p3);
1590 PathFillConvex(col);
1591}
1592
1593void ImDrawList::AddCircle(const ImVec2& center, float radius, ImU32 col, int num_segments, float thickness)
1594{
1595 if ((col & IM_COL32_A_MASK) == 0 || radius < 0.5f)
1596 return;
1597
1598 if (num_segments <= 0)
1599 {
1600 // Use arc with automatic segment count
1601 _PathArcToFastEx(center, radius - 0.5f, 0, IM_DRAWLIST_ARCFAST_SAMPLE_MAX, 0);
1602 _Path.Size--;
1603 }
1604 else
1605 {
1606 // Explicit segment count (still clamp to avoid drawing insanely tessellated shapes)
1607 num_segments = ImClamp(num_segments, 3, IM_DRAWLIST_CIRCLE_AUTO_SEGMENT_MAX);
1608
1609 // Because we are filling a closed shape we remove 1 from the count of segments/points
1610 const float a_max = (IM_PI * 2.0f) * ((float)num_segments - 1.0f) / (float)num_segments;
1611 PathArcTo(center, radius - 0.5f, 0.0f, a_max, num_segments - 1);
1612 }
1613
1614 PathStroke(col, ImDrawFlags_Closed, thickness);
1615}
1616
1617void ImDrawList::AddCircleFilled(const ImVec2& center, float radius, ImU32 col, int num_segments)
1618{
1619 if ((col & IM_COL32_A_MASK) == 0 || radius < 0.5f)
1620 return;
1621
1622 if (num_segments <= 0)
1623 {
1624 // Use arc with automatic segment count
1625 _PathArcToFastEx(center, radius, 0, IM_DRAWLIST_ARCFAST_SAMPLE_MAX, 0);
1626 _Path.Size--;
1627 }
1628 else
1629 {
1630 // Explicit segment count (still clamp to avoid drawing insanely tessellated shapes)
1631 num_segments = ImClamp(num_segments, 3, IM_DRAWLIST_CIRCLE_AUTO_SEGMENT_MAX);
1632
1633 // Because we are filling a closed shape we remove 1 from the count of segments/points
1634 const float a_max = (IM_PI * 2.0f) * ((float)num_segments - 1.0f) / (float)num_segments;
1635 PathArcTo(center, radius, 0.0f, a_max, num_segments - 1);
1636 }
1637
1638 PathFillConvex(col);
1639}
1640
1641// Guaranteed to honor 'num_segments'
1642void ImDrawList::AddNgon(const ImVec2& center, float radius, ImU32 col, int num_segments, float thickness)
1643{
1644 if ((col & IM_COL32_A_MASK) == 0 || num_segments <= 2)
